resolution: Verified fixed in code: src/main/ipc/register-phone-remote.ts now wraps every handler (status/setEnabled/setBind/setPort/setExternalHost/regenerateToken/pairingUrl) in a gated() helper that checks isKnownWindowSender(e.sender) BEFORE touching the service, returning a static REJECTED_STATUS placeholder for status/mutators (never calling svc.status() for a foreign sender, per the finding's own acceptance bar) and { pairingUrl: '' }/{ unavailable: true } for the secret-returning calls. register.ts wires the real wm.isKnownWindowSender in at the composition root (line 226), matching the register-orky-action.ts/register-registry.ts precedent exactly. Dedicated regression coverage exists at tests/main/phone-remote-ipc-gating.test.ts (TEST-2696/2697/2698): a foreign sender is rejected on every channel with the service never touched, and a known-window sender passes through.

claim: Empty/first-run states are blank or bare: an empty pane inventory renders nothing (a dark screen with only an 11px grey connection-status line — no guidance that opening a terminal on the desktop populates it), and a hand-typed URL without a token gets the bare body 'unauthorized' (CONV-001: no hint that the fix is opening the pairing QR from Termhalla Settings — a hint leaks no pane data).
fix: Add an empty-state message to the pane list and make the 401 body actionable, e.g. 'missing or invalid pairing token — open this page from the pairing QR in Termhalla Settings'.
resolution: Verified (REQ-030/REQ-005): an empty inventory renders 'Open a terminal in Termhalla to see it here.' (pane-list.ts render, .pane-list-empty) instead of a blank screen; the 401 body is the actionable 'missing or invalid pairing token — open this page from the pairing QR in Termhalla Settings'; server error frames render in the #error-banner strip. Pinned by TEST-2724 and exercised in DOM e2e TEST-2731 (guidance visible before the first pane spawns).

location: src/main/ipc/register.ts (phoneRemoteService.init before window load); src/main/phone-remote/service.ts attemptStart/status()
claim: The restart-with-port-occupied case the REQ-020 acceptance never covered loses its error entirely: phoneRemoteService.init() runs inside registerHandlers before any renderer has loaded, so an EADDRINUSE at startup fires notifyError into a phoneRemote:changed broadcast no window is yet subscribed to; status() does not carry the error text, so the later seedPhoneRemoteStatus pull sees only enabled:false — the user who had the feature working yesterday relaunches and remote access is silently off with no toast, no message, anywhere. (The in-memory enabled flip is also not persisted on the init-failure path — good for retry-next-launch, but it means the UI shows disabled while quick.json still says enabled, and nothing explains why.)
fix: Carry the last enable-failure message in status() (e.g. status().error) so late-subscribing windows surface it through the same CONV-004 error-severity toast path, or defer/replay the init-failure notification until the first window subscribes.
resolution: The mechanism this finding proposed is implemented and verified: status() now carries the last startup failure (service.ts lastError, cleared on successful start; TEST-2710), an app-wide phoneRemote:error push is consumed at the App.tsx root into the error-severity toast chokepoint, and the Settings section has a not-running/error cue. HOWEVER the current composition still fails to render that carried error in the startup-failure vector (startIfEnabled flips enabled=false so the cue condition enabled&&!running never holds, and the root push fires before any renderer subscribes) — that sharper, current-code defect is re-filed as a successor devils-advocate finding in this round rather than left on this stale claim (whose factual premise, status() does not carry the error, is no longer true).

location: src/phone-client/main.ts handleMessage (no error case); REQ-012/REQ-018 client half
claim: Server error frames are silently discarded by the shipped client: handleMessage has no case for type error (default: break), so the specific, actionable errors REQ-012/REQ-018 require the server to emit — input to an exited/unknown pane, write-failed, malformed-frame codes — never reach the user; typing into a dead pane from the terminal view gives zero feedback while the server dutifully reports the reason. The spec tested error EMISSION exhaustively but never required the client to render any of it — the missing half of CONV-001 (an error surfaced to no one is not surfaced).
fix: Render error frames in the client (e.g. the connection-status line or a transient banner naming code+message), with a DOM-level test that an input-to-exited-pane error becomes visible.
resolution: Verified fixed: the client now has an error case in handleMessage (src/phone-client/main.ts) that renders server error frames into the always-present #error-banner strip via showError (cleared on reconnect), encoded as REQ-030 v2; the actively viewed pane exit additionally renders the in-view process-exited overlay and disables input (TerminalView.setExited). Pinned by the served-DOM e2e TEST-2731 (exit notice) and the REQ-030 client-core coverage; the input-to-exited-pane server error (no-such-pane) is emitted by ws-session doInput and now surfaces in the banner.

claim: The client never unsubscribes: every visited pane stays in the subscribed set forever (back navigation sends nothing, and unsubscribe is never emitted anywhere), so the server keeps streaming data the client drops for non-active panes — battery/data cost on the phone grows with each pane visited, and every reconnect re-subscribes and re-snapshots all of them.
fix: Send unsubscribe for the previous pane on back/switch (or keep only the active pane subscribed), and limit the reconnect attach plan to the panes actually in view.
resolution: Verified (REQ-023 subscription hygiene): paneSwitchPlan unsubscribes the departing pane on every pane switch (main.ts PaneList onSelect) and the back button sends unsubscribe for the active pane before returning to the list; reconnect re-attaches at most the active pane. Pinned by TEST-2722.

claim: service.ts's wss.on('connection', ...) handler (lines ~208-375, ~170 lines) is an undecomposed stateful closure crammed inside createPhoneRemoteService, mixing FOUR distinct concerns inline: (1) a ping/pong keepalive state machine, (2) RST-based forceTerminate fallback logic, (3) a pre-transport burst-queue backpressure bound with its own ad hoc threshold checks against PHONE_WS_HIGH_WATER (lines 342/350/352) that duplicate — rather than delegate to — the ALREADY-EXTRACTED pure BackpressurePolicy from backpressure.ts (which this same connection handler also instantiates separately as stallPolicy for a different purpose), and (4) the armTransportWatch wall-clock pending/stall timer sequencing. None of this per-connection decision logic is unit-testable in isolation; it is exercised only through full real-socket integration tests (TEST-2713/2714), unlike every sibling module this feature built (backpressure.ts, mirror-manager.ts, network-urls.ts, inventory.ts), each a small, cohesive, directly-unit-tested module. This is a clear outlier against both this feature's own established local convention and the project's documented architecture principle (CLAUDE.md: pure logic factored into small pure modules beside each main service so it is unit-testable without Electron; the impure shell kept thin).
fix: Extract the per-connection burst/keepalive/stall state machine into its own factory (e.g. phone-remote/connection-guard.ts) taking {ping, forceTerminate, bufferedAmount, timing} and returning {queueData, dispose}; route the burst-threshold decision through the existing BackpressurePolicy interface (or a documented second instance of it) instead of comparing to PHONE_WS_HIGH_WATER directly in three separate places.

claim: register.ts, the shared IPC composition root for the ENTIRE app (every domain's registrar is composed here), absorbed ~150 lines of phone-remote-specific bridging state and logic in the round-2 loopback: three Maps (phoneRemoteLivePanes, phoneRemoteWorkspaceNames, phoneRemotePaneMeta), five listener Sets, the onPhoneRemoteWorkspaceSaved handler (workspace-save-driven pane-metadata pruning/re-indexing), and the phoneRemotePaneRecord mapping function — growing the file from 213 lines (pre-feature, commit d689673) to 356 (+67%). None of this bridging logic has ANY unit test; grep of tests/ for phoneRemotePaneRecord/phoneRemoteLivePanes/onPhoneRemoteWorkspaceSaved returns zero hits — it is reachable ONLY through the full e2e suite (TEST-2729). This breaks the pattern this exact file already establishes for comparable cross-cutting concerns: OrkyStreamStatusBridge (git status bridging) is its own class, constructed and injected into register.ts, not inlined into it. The phone-remote bridging is the one piece of this feature with zero fast-test coverage, living in the single highest-blast-radius file in the whole IPC layer.
fix: Extract the phone-remote pane/workspace bridging (the three Maps + onWorkspaceSaved handler + phoneRemotePaneRecord mapping) into its own small module (e.g. src/main/phone-remote/pane-bridge.ts) exposing a constructor that returns {onWorkspaceSaved, onSpawn, onResize, wrapSend, panesList}, unit-testable with fake Workspace records the same way backpressure.ts/inventory.ts are — then inject that into register.ts the same way OrkyStreamStatusBridge already is.

claim: Three-plus near-duplicate pane-record type definitions exist across the phone-remote module with no shared canonical type: PhoneRemotePaneRecord (service.ts), RawPaneInfo (inventory.ts), and PhoneRemoteLivePane/PhoneRemotePaneMeta (register.ts) all describe overlapping subsets of the same {paneId, workspaceId, workspaceName, title, kind, cols, rows, status} shape. This forces a no-op identity 'conversion' function in service.ts (toRawPaneInfo: (list: PhoneRemotePaneRecord[]) => RawPaneInfo[] => list, line 148) that exists purely to satisfy TypeScript's structural boundary between two nominally-different-but-identical interfaces. A future field addition to one representation must be manually mirrored across three-plus separate struct definitions with no compiler-enforced link between them.
fix: Hoist one canonical pane-info shape (e.g. into @shared/phone-remote/) and have RawPaneInfo/PhoneRemotePaneRecord both alias or extend it; drop the toRawPaneInfo identity function.

claim: PhoneRemoteSettings.tsx's doc comment claims its architecture — a component-local createPhoneRemoteSlice instance instead of the app-wide zustand store every other Settings section uses (compare GeneralSettings.tsx, which reads exclusively via useStore(s => s.quick.X)) — is 'a deliberate, documented scope boundary, see the feature's implementation notes' (lines 18-20). This is false: grep of docs/features/phone-web-remote.md and docs/deferred.md for any mention of a component-local slice, zustand, app-wide store, or this scope decision returns zero hits; the 'Deferred follow-ons' section lists five unrelated items and says nothing about this. This is the SAME false-documentation-claim pattern already found and fixed once in this feature (the original FINDING-011: a register.ts comment claiming 'tracked as a documented follow-on' with neither doc mentioning it) — recurring here, in a different file, after the very loopback that was supposed to fix this feature's documentation discipline.
fix: Either add the component-local-slice scope-boundary note to docs/features/phone-web-remote.md (a natural fit for the existing 'Deferred follow-ons' section or a new architecture note), or remove the false 'documented' claim from the comment.

claim: The v2 pre-transport burst queue does per-chunk work for panes a session is not even tracking: service.ts:427 fans EVERY pane's every data chunk into EVERY connected session's queueData, which allocates a {paneId,chunk} entry, retains the chunk string until the next tick, and runs an O(chunk-length) Buffer.byteLength scan — even for a session sitting on the pane list with zero subscriptions, whose flushBurst then hands each chunk to session.paneData only to drop it at the live/attaching/resyncing check (a guaranteed no-op round trip; the v1 direct path dropped these O(1) with no scan/alloc). Worse, untracked panes' bytes count toward burstBytes, so a synchronous >1MiB flood from a pane NOBODY subscribes to trips burstSaturated and discards the whole queue, marking any subscribed pane that co-emitted in that tick stale — forcing a full snapshot serialize + resync cycle the real transport never required (the socket may be idle). Correctness is preserved (the resync is exact), but this is unnecessary hot-path work and spurious resync churn caused by irrelevant traffic.
fix: Expose an isTracking(paneId) (live || attaching || resyncing) on WsSession and have queueData skip chunks for untracked panes entirely — their delivery decision is already a guaranteed drop — so burst-byte accounting reflects only send-eligible bytes and an unrelated pane's flood can neither saturate the burst nor stale a watched pane.

claim: The v2 inventory re-push path (REQ-011 currency) does redundant N+1 work with no change detection: pushInventoryToAll delegates to each session's sendInventory, which independently calls deps.panes.inventory() — a full list() materialization + buildInventory grouping + JSON stringify PER SESSION per push, where one build fanned to all sessions would do. And onPhoneRemoteWorkspaceSaved (register.ts:83-96) fires the membership listeners unconditionally on EVERY workspace save — including metadata-neutral saves (a maximize/minimize toggle or layout drag rides the workspace record per the persisted view-state design), so each such desktop gesture re-runs ensureTap over the full pane list (service.ts:443), rebuilds the inventory S times, sends a full inventory frame to every connected phone (cellular bandwidth for a no-op), and triggers PaneList.setInventory's full DOM teardown/rebuild client-side.
fix: Build the inventory ONCE per push and fan the identical payload to every session; fingerprint the built inventory (e.g. JSON string equality against the last pushed value) and skip the push + client re-render when unchanged, so only genuine membership/metadata changes reach the wire.

claim: REQ-009/REQ-017 exactly-once broken by the round-2 welded pre-transport burst queue: service.ts queueData/flushBurst defers EVERY session.paneData delivery by one setTimeout(0) macrotask while the shared mirror is fed synchronously at onData time (wireDataSources: tap(chunk) then entry.queueData, lines 424-427), desynchronizing the mirror write barrier from the session hold-windows. (a) DUPLICATION: a chunk already fed to the mirror but still parked in the burst queue when a subscribe (ws message -> doSubscribe) or a pong-driven socketDrained captures its snapshot barrier is included in the snapshot AND then flushed into the attach/resync pending queue and re-delivered as data — the client renders it twice, violating REQ-009 (no byte is delivered both in the snapshot and again as live data ... never duplicated) and the REQ-017 replace-then-append reconstruction. The race window is per-chunk (one macrotask) and recurs continuously on a busy pane, so subscribing to a streaming pane hits it with real probability; both snapshot-resolution orders duplicate. (b) LOSS: when a burst crosses PHONE_WS_HIGH_WATER, real chunks are discarded and each affected pane is marked stale via the session.paneData(id, EMPTY-STRING) hack (lines 341-346 and 351-357) — but ws-session.paneData checks attaching/resyncing BEFORE live (lines 196-201), so for a pane inside an attach/resync hold-window the empty chunk is queued into the window and policy.onData is never consulted: no staleness is recorded, the dropped bytes are post-barrier (excluded from the already-captured snapshot), and no resync ever repairs them — permanent client divergence, violating REQ-009 (never dropped) and REQ-017 (marks its subscribed panes stale). No frozen test can see either direction: TEST-2625/2705/2706 drive createWsSession directly, bypassing the burst queue (the harness comment even models the pre-weld synchronous feed order), and the service-level TEST-2648 vectors never interleave output within the one-macrotask burst window.
fix: Make barrier capture and hold-window entry share one ordering domain with the burst queue: (1) synchronously flush the connection burst queue BEFORE any snapshot barrier is captured — thread a flushNow() dep into the session, invoked at the top of doSubscribe and socketDrained (or route barrier capture through the same queue); (2) replace the paneData(id, EMPTY-STRING) hack with an explicit session.markStale(paneId) that records staleness in the policy even while the pane is attaching/resyncing (applied when the window completes); (3) add service-level interleaved vectors: output fed and subscribe sent within the same tick asserting exactly-once reconstruction, and a saturation burst during an in-flight attach/resync asserting the dropped bytes are eventually resynced. CONV-060 class — the amended REQ-009/REQ-017 interleaving acceptance must be exercised through the REAL service seam, not only the session harness.
location: src/main/phone-remote/service.ts:319-358 (queueData/flushBurst) vs ws-session.ts:145/196-201/244 (snapshot barrier capture + hold-window entry)
resolution: Verified fixed in commit c39dba3 by code-trace of both defect halves through src/main/phone-remote/service.ts + ws-session.ts + backpressure.ts + replay-engine.ts. (a) DUPLICATION: every snapshot-barrier-capturing entry point now synchronously drains the connection's burst queue first — ws.on('message') calls flushNow() before session.handleFrame (service.ts:409, so doSubscribe's barrier can never cover a still-parked chunk), ws.on('pong') calls flushNow() before socketDrained() (service.ts:322, same for the resync barrier), and the paneExit fan-out flushes each entry before session.paneExit (service.ts:475, so a parked final chunk is delivered, not discarded). The replay engine captures its barrier synchronously at snapshot() call time (target = issuedSeq, replay-engine.ts:119), so post-flush chunks are excluded from the snapshot and later flushed exactly once as data — traced the finding's interleavings in both snapshot-resolution orders plus attach/resync supersession (identity guards make an orphaned window's chunks land inside the superseding barrier). (b) LOSS: the paneData(id,'') substitute is gone; queueData's saturation discard calls session.markStale (service.ts:381/389), which records staleness through an attach/resync hold window (ws-session.ts:244-247 accepts live OR attaching OR resyncing; policy.markStale is direct); finishAttach/finishResync's deliverData then drops the stale pane's pending chunks (all mirror-covered) and the pong-driven next drain opens a resync whose fresh barrier covers the discarded bytes; socketDrained additionally refuses to open a resync window over an in-flight attach (ws-session.ts:259, re-marks stale) so a resync buffer-replace can never erase attach-delivered bytes. armTransportWatch is armed on every markStale so the repairing drain actually occurs. TEST-2713 exercises the live-pane saturation-to-resync path through the real transport. Residual: the fix's demanded service-level interleaved vectors (barrier race, mid-window saturation) were NOT added — test-freeze held through the fix round; that unpinned-repair gap is already tracked as FINDING-079 (networking) and FINDING-077 (devils-advocate), not re-filed.

claim: Residual host-locale dependence inside the FINDING-006 fix: rankReachableUrls tiebreaks within a rank via a.name.localeCompare(b.name) || a.address.localeCompare(b.address). localeCompare with no locale argument uses the runtime default locale/ICU data, so the within-rank order (and therefore which address becomes the top-ranked QR pairing host when two adapters share an RFC1918 class) can differ across machines with different OS display languages or Node ICU builds, and can change on the SAME machine after a locale change — a smaller instance of the nondeterministic-selection class REQ-031 was written to close. TEST-2694 cannot catch this: permutation invariance holds under ANY fixed comparator, and its ASCII fixture names order identically in common locales.
fix: Use locale-independent code-unit comparison for both tiebreaks (a.name < b.name ? -1 : a.name > b.name ? 1 : 0, same for address — or localeCompare with an explicit fixed locale such as en and numeric options) so the ranking is a pure function of the interface table on every host.

claim: The v2 pre-transport burst queue breaks REQ-009/REQ-017 exactly-once in two interleavings, because it defers session fan-in by a macrotask (setTimeout(flushBurst, 0)) while the mirror is fed synchronously (register.ts onData: tap(chunk) BEFORE entry.queueData). (1) DUPLICATION: a chunk fed just before a subscribe sits in burstQueue while the subscribe frame is processed (doSubscribe opens the attach window and requests mirrors.snapshot(), whose write-flush barrier INCLUDES that chunk — replay-engine.ts: 'reflecting every byte fed BEFORE this call'); when flushBurst then runs, session.paneData finds the pane attaching and pushes the same chunk into the attach pending queue, so finishAttach delivers it AGAIN as data after the snapshot that already contains it. REQ-009 MUST: 'no byte is delivered both in the snapshot and again as live data', explicitly 'repeated with output racing the subscribe call'. The same gap applies to a resync window (finishResync). (2) SILENT LOSS: when burstSaturated trips while a pane is in an attaching/resyncing hold window, the queued real chunks are discarded but the stale-marking substitute session.paneData(id, '') short-circuits into the pending array (ws-session.ts:196-200) WITHOUT ever reaching deliverData/policy.onData — so the pane is never marked stale, no resync ever repairs it, and bytes fed after the snapshot call are permanently missing from the client (REQ-009 'never dropped' / REQ-017 'no delivered byte is erased'). The session-level tests (TEST-2625/2705/2706) all drive session.paneData directly and never traverse the burst layer, so neither vector is pinned.
fix: Make the burst layer hold-window-aware: flush (or hand off) a connection's queued chunks for a pane synchronously BEFORE the session processes a subscribe for that pane (e.g. route inbound frames through a wrapper that drains burstQueue entries for msg.paneId first), or timestamp/sequence chunks so finishAttach/finishResync drop pending entries already covered by the snapshot barrier. For the loss half: when burstSaturated discards a queue, mark affected panes stale through the policy even when they are mid-attach/mid-resync (e.g. add a session.forceStale(paneId) that finishAttach/finishResync consult to trigger an immediate follow-up resync). Add service-level tests through the REAL connection path: subscribe racing a same-tick burst chunk reconstructs exactly-once, and a >HIGH_WATER synchronous flood during an in-flight attach ends with a resync that restores the flooded bytes.
resolution: Verified fixed in commit c39dba3 by reading the code: (1) duplication half — ws.on('message') (service.ts:404-411) invokes flushNow() BEFORE session.handleFrame, so a subscribe's snapshot barrier (doSubscribe -> mirrors.snapshot) can no longer be captured while a mirror-fed chunk is still parked in the burst queue, and ws.on('pong') (service.ts:316-328) invokes flushNow() before session.socketDrained() for the resync barrier — traced both interleavings: a same-tick parked chunk now lands in the snapshot exactly once (flushed pre-barrier it is dropped-if-unsubscribed or delivered-then-replaced; parked post-barrier it rides the hold window exactly once). (2) loss half — the saturation discard calls session.markStale(paneId) (service.ts:381,389) instead of paneData(id,''), ws-session.markStale (ws-session.ts:244-247) records staleness in the policy even while the pane is attaching/resyncing (backpressure.ts:98-100 markStale added), socketDrained keeps a still-attaching stale pane stale rather than opening a concurrent resync window (ws-session.ts:259, no delivered-then-erased hazard), and the next drain resyncs from mirror truth (mirror fed synchronously before the discard). paneExit additionally flushes parked chunks before subscription state is cleared (service.ts:475). socketDrained has no other caller (grepped). Residual gap — no test pins this ordering (fix round was test-frozen) — filed separately as a MEDIUM.

claim: No Origin header validation exists on either the WS upgrade (service.ts handleUpgrade) or the authenticated HTTP routes (handleRequest), and REQ-028's new HttpOnly session cookie is exactly the kind of ambient credential Cross-Site WebSocket Hijacking (CSWSH) targets: a malicious page loaded in the same phone browser (e.g. reachable on the same LAN/tailnet, or via a link) can open new WebSocket('ws://<host>:<port>/ws') and, if any current or future browser fails to withhold a SameSite=Lax cookie on that cross-site WS handshake (the sole mitigation in place -- iOS Safari in particular has a history of inconsistent SameSite enforcement, and the WS-handshake-is-subject-to-SameSite behavior is a browser implementation detail nowhere pinned by a spec REQ or test in this feature), the attacker's page rides the victim's already-authenticated session to read every pane's live output and type into any pane. Token-based auth (query/header) is unaffected since the token is never ambient, but the cookie path added in v2 has no second layer of defense -- no Origin/Host allowlist check anywhere in auth.ts or service.ts, and no test exercises a cross-origin Origin header.
fix: In handleUpgrade (and optionally handleRequest for cookie-authenticated requests), when the request carries an Origin header, reject the upgrade/request unless Origin's host:port matches the server's own bind host:port (or the configured externalHost) -- mirroring the defense-in-depth pattern already used for IPC sender gating (REQ-032) applied to this feature's other ambient-credential surface. Absent-Origin requests (non-browser WS clients using the token) are unaffected. Add a unit test asserting a cookie-authenticated WS upgrade/HTTP request with a foreign Origin header is refused.

claim: A connected client's knowledge of an UNSUBSCRIBED pane's grid goes stale with no refresh path, so a later tap renders the snapshot mis-wrapped — the exact outcome REQ-013 exists to prevent. paneGrid drops the push unless the pane is live/attaching (ws-session.ts:214), a desktop grid change re-pushes no inventory (only spawn/exit/ws:save do, and a window resize changes no persisted layout so no ws:save fires), and the attach sequence sends no grid message (finishAttach emits snapshot only). Vector: phone connects, desktop window is resized (every pane's cols/rows change; mirrors ARE resized via mirrors.resizePane, service.ts:435-438), user taps a pane — openPanePlan sizes the terminal from the STALE inventory row, then the snapshot (serialized at the NEW grid) renders re-wrapped, and no grid push ever follows because the resize already happened before the pane became attaching. Unrepaired until the next desktop resize or a reconnect. Spec origin: REQ-011's currency list covers status and membership but not grid changes, and REQ-013 scopes grid pushes to 'attached clients' — no requirement keeps an unattached pane's advertised grid fresh, so no frozen test demanded it (the REQ-013 acceptance taps a pane whose inventory was fresh at connect time).
fix: Have the server emit a grid{paneId,cols,rows} message as the first step of the attach sequence, ahead of the snapshot (finishAttach knows nothing of grids today — thread the pane's current cols/rows through WsSessionDeps.panes, e.g. an info(paneId) accessor, and send grid before snapshot; the client's setGrid-on-grid-push path already handles it before data renders). This closes the staleness class for ALL causes (resize while unsubscribed, missed pushes, reconnect) in one place. Alternatively re-push the inventory on grid change, coalesced. Amend REQ-013/REQ-011 acceptance with the vector: resize a pane on the desktop while a client is connected but NOT subscribed to it, then tap it — the client terminal must be at the new grid before the snapshot applies.

claim: REQ-020 violation: a startup failure occurring before any window has loaded still surfaces NOWHERE user-visible. phoneRemoteService.init() runs inside registerHandlers, which src/main/index.ts explicitly orders BEFORE wm.start() loads any renderer — so notifyError's phoneRemote:error/phoneRemote:changed pushes fire into not-yet-loaded webContents and are lost; no root consumer ever pulls status() at window load (App.tsx only subscribes the push), and the ONE surface that reads status().error — PhoneRemoteSettings — renders it only under notRunning = enabled && !running, a state the service makes unreachable in any settled state: startIfEnabled coerces enabled=false on every failed start. Net effect: with persisted enabled:true and the port occupied at startup, the amended acceptance's 'the first loaded window still surfaces the error' is unmet — the user sees an unchecked toggle with zero explanation (opening Settings > Phone shows nothing: seedPhoneRemoteStatus deliberately does not toast, and the notRunning gate is false), and the same dead gate makes REQ-029's 'not-running cue plus error text whenever enabled is true but running is false' render path vacuous (TEST-2710 pins only the status()-carries half; TEST-2717 is a source-grep that cannot see reachability). This is the unfixed renderer half of open FINDING-049 and the reason FINDING-043 remains functionally true in production.
fix: (a) At renderer root load (App.tsx mount effects), pull phoneRemoteStatus() once and route a present status().error through pushToast(..., 'error') — the late-subscribing-window report REQ-020 demands; (b) render the panel's error line whenever status().error is present (key the cue on error/running, not on enabled), or stop coercing enabled=false on failure so the REQ-029 state is reachable; add a composition-level test: occupied port + persisted enabled:true at startup yields a visible toast/panel indication in the first loaded window.
resolution: Verified fixed in code, all three halves: (a) service.ts startIfEnabled (lines 503-515) no longer coerces enabled=false on a failed start - enabled is kept as the user's persistent intent, so status() reports the { enabled: true, running: false, error } shape and the REQ-029 notRunning gate is REACHABLE (restart() also lets an enabled-but-errored service retry on a port/bind change); (b) PhoneRemoteSettings.tsx:114-118 renders the role=alert not-running cue with the specific status().error text keyed on enabled && !running; (c) App.tsx:166-168 performs a root-load recovery pull of phoneRemoteStatus() and routes a carried error through pushToast('error') - recovering the pre-window push that webContents.send dropped - alongside the always-subscribed onPhoneRemoteError push (App.tsx:111). TEST-2710 pins the status()-carries half (occupied port at init => running:false + error naming the port). Residual: the composed acceptance vector (persisted enabled:true + occupied port => visible indication in the first loaded window; enabled stays true) is pinned by NO test - filed separately as a new MEDIUM.

claim: CONV-038 violation: one failed enable click raises FOUR identical error toasts, none of which auto-dismiss (error-kind toasts persist until hand-dismissed; MAX_TOASTS=4, so the entire toast stack fills with copies). The uncoordinated paths for the single failure moment: (1) service notifyError -> phoneRemote:error -> App.tsx root pushToast; (2) notifyError's phoneRemote:changed carrying error -> the mounted panel's ingestPhoneRemoteStatus -> applyStatus toast; (3) the registrar's broadcast() re-sends phoneRemote:changed whose status() still carries lastError -> a second ingest toast; (4) the invoke's returned status -> the slice's own applyStatus toast. Compounding it, applyStatus toasts on ANY status carrying error (not on an error transition) and lastError is cleared only by a successful start — so a later unrelated op (setExternalHost, setBind) re-raises the stale failure as a fresh error toast. The slice comment acknowledges only a two-path belt-and-suspenders echo; no dedupe/coexistence policy exists for the four actual paths.
fix: Pick one owner for the failure toast (the App-root phoneRemote:error push) and make the slice's applyStatus stop toasting, or toast only on an error TRANSITION (track the previous status.error); do not re-broadcast an error-carrying status from the registrar when notifyError already pushed the same message.

location: src/main/phone-remote/ws-session.ts:123-131,213-217 (no grid on attach; grid gated on live/attaching) + src/main/ipc/register.ts:172-176 (onResize fires no membership push)
claim: REQ-013's stated goal ('a non-80x24 pane never renders mis-wrapped') fails in the most common interleaving: a desktop resize while the phone is NOT attached to that pane (any desktop window resize refits EVERY pane via the FitAddon). Grid pushes are subscription-gated (ws-session.paneGrid returns unless live/attaching), a resize triggers no inventory re-push (register.ts onResize fires only grid listeners; REQ-011's currency set enumerates status and membership only), and attach sends no grid with the snapshot (finishAttach emits snapshot+queued data only) — so the client's freshest KNOWN grid (its last-pushed inventory row) is stale, the tap sizes xterm at the old cols/rows, the snapshot (serialized from the mirror, which IS resized at the true grid) renders mis-wrapped, and nothing ever corrects it until the desktop next resizes that specific pane. The reconnect re-open path has the same defect: main.ts re-opens the active pane in the ws open handler from the PRE-disconnect inventory, before the fresh panes message arrives. Each individual REQ is met to the letter; the composite defeats the normative intent — a gap in the spec's REQ-011 currency enumeration that no acceptance vector exercises (all grid vectors resize while a client is attached).
fix: Send a grid message (from the server-side inventory's/mirror's current cols/rows) immediately BEFORE the snapshot in finishAttach — one change covering both select and reconnect — and/or add grid changes to the REQ-011 currency push set; add a vector: resize a pane while the client is on the list view, tap the pane, assert the client terminal's grid equals the NEW size before snapshot application.

claim: The phone client's #error-banner is sticky: clearError is called ONLY in the WS open handler, so a one-off server error frame (input to a pane that just exited, an unknown-message notice) stays rendered for the remainder of the session — across pane switches, back navigation, and successful later input — with no dismiss affordance and no timeout. A stale red banner describing a long-gone condition reads as a live problem and desensitizes the user to the banner that also carries genuinely important states.
fix: Clear the banner on pane switch/back navigation and on the next successful interaction (or add tap-to-dismiss / a timeout for transient error frames), keeping terminal states persistent.

claim: The Ctrl latch's armed state is conveyed by background color alone (.key-bar-key--active swaps #2a2a2a for #3a6ea5) with no aria-pressed on the toggle button and no non-color cue — the Ctrl key IS a stateful toggle (arm, then the next letter becomes a control byte) but is announced identically to the stateless keys, so a screen-reader user cannot tell whether the latch is armed and a color-blind user must distinguish two mid-luminance fills to know whether the next keystroke will be transformed.
fix: Set aria-pressed to the latch state on the Ctrl button (both toggle sites already read keyBar.isCtrlLatched()) and add a non-color cue (border/underline/dot) to the --active style.

claim: CONV-001/CONV-002 class: the external-host field accepts any string silently — a value carrying a scheme, slash, port, or embedded space (e.g. https://my.ts.net or host:8080, both plausible paste mistakes for exactly this field's tailscale use case) is trimmed, persisted as-is, and interpolated into buildPairingUrl as http://<value>:<port>/?token=..., yielding a syntactically broken pairing URL and a QR that scans to garbage, with no validation message or error anywhere on the desktop; the only symptom is a phone that cannot connect, with nothing pointing back at the malformed host.
fix: Validate the entry as a bare hostname on apply (reject scheme/slash/colon/whitespace with a specific role=alert message mirroring the port field's pattern), or normalize by URL-parsing and extracting the hostname; add malformed-input vectors for setExternalHost.

claim: CONV-008 class doc contradiction: the History-limits section states a dropped WS needs no re-pairing because 'the client retries with its stored token' — contradicting the v2 model the SAME document explains above it (the HttpOnly session cookie is the durable client credential; REQ-023 forbids persisting the plaintext token in script-readable storage, pinned by TEST-2672 and by main.ts, which stores no token). A user or maintainer reading the reconnection section is told the phone stores the pairing token — exactly the property the ESC-001 amendment removed.
fix: Reword to: the client retries and authenticates via its HttpOnly session cookie (sent automatically on the same-origin upgrade); per CONV-008, grep the whole docs tree (plus CLAUDE.md) for other stored-token phrasings of the pre-amendment model.

claim: REQ-028 MUST is conditional in the implementation: the Set-Cookie is issued only when `auth.viaToken && sessionToken !== undefined`, but sessionToken is in-memory-only and dies with the app session — so after ANY desktop restart, a token-authenticated `GET /?token=<still-valid>` (the persisted tokenHash validates it) returns 200 with NO cookie. The spec mandates unconditionally that 'the first token-authenticated HTTP response MUST set a persistent session cookie'. Consequence: a pairing URL captured through the REQ-031 copyable-URL channel (iMessage/notes) or lingering in Safari history and first opened AFTER a desktop restart loads the shell, strips the token from the URL (REQ-023), then the cookie-less token-less WS upgrade is 401-refused; after 3 quick refusals the client shows the terminal 'pairing revoked — scan the new QR code' state — while the token is actually VALID and Settings says '(paired)' and 'already-paired phones keep working'. TEST-2692 covers cookie ACCEPTANCE after restart but no test covers cookie ISSUANCE after restart, so the gap is invisible to the suite. The condition is also needless: the presented token was just verified against tokenHash — it IS the plaintext the cookie needs.
fix: Issue the cookie from the PRESENTED, just-verified token instead of (or falling back to) sessionToken: have authorizeRequest return the verified plaintext credential and pass that to issueSetCookie; add a TEST-2692-style vector asserting a fresh service instance loading only persisted state sets the cookie on a token-authenticated GET, and that the subsequent token-less WS upgrade with only that cookie succeeds.
resolution: Verified in code (round-3 commit c39dba3): auth.ts authorizeRequest now returns the PRESENTED, just-verified plaintext on the token path ({authorized, viaToken, token}), and service.ts handleRequest (lines 201-211) issues the Set-Cookie from auth.token — never from the in-memory sessionToken — so a valid pairing URL first opened after a desktop restart (sessionToken undefined, tokenHash persisted) still yields the cookie. Traced the vector end-to-end: fresh instance loads only persisted tokenHash, GET /?token=<valid> verifies via verifyToken(token, tokenHash), auth.viaToken=true with auth.token set, issueSetCookie(auth.token) emits the HttpOnly cookie whose value verifies against the same persisted hash. Regenerate-revokes (REQ-006) and no-new-persisted-secret (REQ-004) are preserved since validity stays a pure function of tokenHash. The requested regression pin (issuance-after-restart vector) was NOT added (frozen suite unchanged at the fix round) — filed separately as a test-adequacy finding rather than holding this defect finding open.

claim: The REQ-020 startup-failure vector is still dead in production despite the v2 amendment: on any failed start, startIfEnabled flips settings.enabled to false, so status() can NEVER report the { enabled: true, running: false } shape — which is the ONLY condition under which PhoneRemoteSettings renders its not-running cue and the status().error text (notRunning = enabled && !running). For a startup failure (persisted enabled:true, port occupied), the app-wide phoneRemote:error push fires inside registerHandlers before any renderer has loaded/subscribed (webContents.send to an unloaded window is dropped), and seedPhoneRemoteStatus deliberately does not toast — so the carried status().error is rendered NOWHERE: the user relaunches into a silently-off feature with the Settings checkbox unchecked (while quick.json still says enabled:true), '(paired)' shown, and no explanation anywhere. This violates the REQ-020 acceptance ('with persisted enabled:true and the port occupied at app startup, the first loaded window still surfaces the error') and makes the REQ-029 errored-state acceptance ('with enabled:true and a stopped/errored server the section shows the not-running cue and the error text') satisfiable only with a status shape the real service can never produce — and its pin, TEST-2717, is a SOURCE-SCAN of the component file (the exact reachability anti-pattern REQ-029 itself bans), so the suite cannot see it. Recovery exists only by accident: re-toggling Enable re-fails and toasts. This is the third round of exactly this defect class (FINDING-034/049 lineage).
fix: Keep the user intent and the failure state distinguishable: do not flip enabled on a failed start (report enabled:true, running:false, error) or derive the Settings cue from status.error presence rather than enabled&&!running; AND replay the last error to late subscribers — e.g. the root App.tsx consumer pulls status() once on mount and pushes status.error through the error-severity toast chokepoint (or main re-broadcasts phoneRemote:error on each window did-finish-load). Pin with a test that drives the REAL service to a startup failure and asserts the rendered surface (not a source scan). propose CONV: A status field added so late subscribers can report an earlier failure MUST have a pinned consumer that renders it in the exact state the failure produces — a carrier with no reachable renderer is not surfacing.
resolution: Verified in code (round-3 commit c39dba3), all three halves: (1) service.ts startIfEnabled (lines 503-515) no longer coerces enabled=false on a failed start — enabled is kept as persistent user intent, so status() reports the exact { enabled: true, running: false, error } shape; restart() (517-530) additionally falls through for enabled-but-errored so a setPort/setBind actually retries the start (the error's own corrective hint now works); doSetEnabled's idempotency guard (enabled===listening && enabled===settings.enabled) still permits an explicit retry and a clean disable from the errored state. (2) PhoneRemoteSettings.tsx renders the not-running cue + the specific status().error text under notRunning = enabled && !running (lines 77, 114-118) — now a REACHABLE state. (3) App.tsx (lines 159-168) pulls phoneRemoteStatus() once at root load and routes a carried status().error through the store toast chokepoint with the error severity (missed-push recovery for the pre-window phoneRemote:error push), satisfying the REQ-020 'first loaded window still surfaces the error' acceptance. Residuals filed separately: the requested real-vector pin was not added (frozen suite unchanged — test-adequacy finding), and lastError is never cleared on explicit disable so the root pull can ghost-toast a stale error (new LOW).

location: 02-spec.md REQ-022/REQ-028 (standalone relaunch via cookie); src/phone-client/manifest.webmanifest start_url
claim: The flagship install story rests on an unanalyzed platform assumption: REQ-022 promises 'a standalone relaunch from the home screen authenticates via the REQ-028 session cookie', but iOS home-screen web apps have historically run in their OWN storage container, separate from Safari — a cookie set during the Safari pairing visit is not necessarily present in the standalone container, so the FIRST launch of the installed PWA (start_url ./, token-free by design) may receive the plaintext 401 with no recovery path inside the app (standalone mode has no address bar to re-enter a tokened URL, and the QR opens Safari, not the PWA). The spec never analyzes this container-isolation behavior, and no CI test can: every acceptance vector for REQ-022/REQ-028 runs server-side or in Chromium, where cookies are trivially shared — the one environment that matters (iOS Safari -> Add to Home Screen -> standalone launch) is exactly the one the suite cannot reach. If isolation holds on current iOS, the iPhone-Safari-PWA story degrades to Safari-tab-only while every test stays green and the docs keep claiming home-screen relaunch works.
fix: Verify on-device (current iOS: pair in Safari, install, cold-launch standalone) and encode the result: if the cookie does not carry over, either document the limitation (use from a Safari tab / re-pair flow) in docs/features/phone-web-remote.md and the pairing UI, or add an in-app recovery path reachable from the 401 (e.g. the 401 page offers a token-entry form posting to a cookie-setting endpoint — secret-free until a valid token is presented). Record the on-device verification (or the accepted risk) in the feature review-followups doc so the claim is not silently untested.

location: src/main/phone-remote/ws-session.ts:213-217 (paneGrid subscription gate) + src/phone-client/main.ts:81-89 (open from inventory row); REQ-013 interleaving
claim: REQ-013 is met to the letter but its stated purpose ('a non-80x24 pane never renders mis-wrapped') fails in a plausible interleaving the spec and tests ignore: session.paneGrid drops grid pushes for panes the client is not subscribed to/attaching, and the inventory rows' cols/rows refresh only on membership pushes (spawn/exit/ws:save) — so a desktop grid change that produces NO workspace autosave (window resize, font zoom, sidebar toggle: layout percentages unchanged, every pane refit) while the phone sits on the LIST view leaves every row's grid stale. Tapping a pane then sizes the terminal from the stale row (openPanePlan), the snapshot arrives serialized at the NEW grid, and the pane renders permanently garbled — no later grid push ever arrives (the next one fires only on the NEXT desktop resize while subscribed), and back-and-retap reads the same stale row. The mitigation that exists (splitter-drag resizes ride the autosave -> membership re-push) is incidental coupling, not design: the attach path itself never delivers the authoritative grid the server holds. Every REQ-013 acceptance vector seeds a FRESH inventory, so the stale-row interleaving is untested.
fix: Make attach authoritative: on subscribe, send the pane's current grid (a grid message emitted in finishAttach before the snapshot, or a cols/rows field on the snapshot message) so the client always re-sizes from the server's truth immediately before applying the snapshot; alternatively ungate paneGrid (status pushes already flow unsubscribed and grid is the same size class). Add an interleaving test: connect, receive inventory, resize the pane server-side WITHOUT a membership push, tap, assert the rendered grid matches the mirror grid.

claim: The shipped feature doc contradicts the shipped v2 design in two places: (1) the Backpressure section still says the resync is 'driven by the transport's own drain event' — the ws library's WebSocket emits no drain event (the exact FINDING-030 defect the v2 rework removed); the real mechanism is the pong-gated socketDrained plus the armed-while-pending transport watch. (2) the History-limits section says a reconnecting client 'retries with its stored token' — directly contradicting the cookie model documented two sections earlier and REQ-023's MUST that the plaintext token is never in script-readable storage (the client stores NO token; the HttpOnly cookie is the durable credential). Both are leftover v1 sentences; REQ-027 requires the doc to document the actual security/pairing model, and a future maintainer reading either sentence would re-derive the wrong design.
fix: Amend docs/features/phone-web-remote.md: describe the pong-gated drain trigger + armed transport watch (and the burst-queue bound) in the Backpressure section, and replace 'retries with its stored token' with the cookie-credential reconnect description.

claim: Four exported/public members in the phone-remote module have zero production call sites — dead code the shipped feature never exercises, one of it actively misleading: (1) reconnectAttachPlan (src/phone-client/ws-client.ts:36-38) is documented 'a reconnect performs a FRESH REQ-009 attach per subscribed pane' and is unit-tested (tests/phone-client-core.test.ts TEST-2674, asserting a 2-pane plan) as if it were the client's live reconnect mechanism, but main.ts's actual reconnect handler (the WS 'open' listener, ~line 187) re-attaches only activePaneId via TerminalView.open/openPanePlan — a materially different, narrower behavior born of the later subscription-hygiene pivot (FINDING-003/028: at most one pane is ever subscribed). The passing TEST-2674 gives false confidence that multi-pane reconnect re-attach is a live, tested behavior; it is not reachable from any entry point. (2) TerminalView.setPane (terminal-view.ts:88-90) has an inline comment claiming it is 'kept for callers that only need to redirect input' but has zero callers anywhere in src/ or tests/. (3) PhoneRemoteServer.isRunning() (server.ts:24,87) was floated as one alternative fix for FINDING-018 ('setEnabled idempotent... transport.isRunning()') but the fix that actually shipped took the other documented alternative (start() returns the existing bound port) — isRunning() was never wired to anything and has no dedicated test file. (4) staticFilePath (static-assets.ts:48-51), whose own doc comment claims it exists for 'tests/diagnostics', has zero call sites in tests/ or src/.
fix: Delete all four (reconnectAttachPlan + its TEST-2674 unit test, TerminalView.setPane, PhoneRemoteServer.isRunning, staticFilePath) unless a real caller is added. If reconnectAttachPlan's multi-pane semantics are intentionally retained for a future multi-subscription mode, say so explicitly in its doc comment and mark TEST-2674 as pinning a not-yet-wired capability rather than implying it is live REQ-024 behavior.

claim: The FINDING-063/071 fix (the REQ-020/REQ-029 errored-state surfacing) is unpinned against exactly the regression that already happened once between review rounds 1 and 2: no test asserts that settings.enabled SURVIVES a failed start (TEST-2710 asserts running:false and error text but never status().enabled - reintroducing the old startIfEnabled enabled=false coercion passes the entire frozen suite while silently killing the panel's notRunning gate and the checkbox state again); no test renders PhoneRemoteSettings with an errored status or seeds an errored server through the real composition (TEST-2717 is a source-grep of the component file - the class TEST-2716's own header warns cannot see reachability; TEST-2730 opens Settings against a healthy seam-fixed server only); and App.tsx's root-load recovery pull (the REQ-020 'first loaded window still surfaces the error' acceptance half) is asserted nowhere (app-queue-wiring.test.ts has no phoneRemote coverage, TEST-2718 pins only the push subscription). REQ-020's amended acceptance ('with persisted enabled: true and the port occupied at app startup, the first loaded window still surfaces the error') and REQ-029's ('with enabled: true and a stopped/errored server the section shows the not-running cue and the error text from status()') therefore have no executable vector - a shallow-pin gap on a twice-regressed behavior, not a behavior defect (the current code is verified correct).
fix: Extend TEST-2710 to assert status().enabled === true after the failed start (one line, pins the enabled-intent invariant that gates every renderer surface), and add either (a) a component render test mounting PhoneRemoteSettings with an injected { enabled: true, running: false, error } status asserting the role=alert cue and error text are in the DOM, or (b) an e2e vector that occupies the seam-fixed port with persisted enabled:true and asserts the first window shows the error toast and the Settings section shows the not-running cue. Requires a tests-phase amendment per the freeze discipline.

claim: Every ESC-003 fix shipped with ZERO regression pins: the round-3 implement gate passed with the frozen suite byte-identical, so the exact vectors that produced this feature's three rounds of same-class regressions are still untested. Concretely: (a) FINDING-070's vector — cookie ISSUANCE after a desktop restart (fresh service instance loading only persisted tokenHash, GET /?token=<valid> must respond Set-Cookie) — TEST-2691 pins in-session issuance and TEST-2692 pins post-restart ACCEPTANCE, but no test composes the two, so reverting the auth.token change back to sessionToken-conditional issuance passes the whole suite; (b) FINDING-071/063's vector — a failed start must keep status().enabled === true — TEST-2653/TEST-2710 assert running:false and the error text but never assert enabled, so re-adding the enabled=false coercion (which re-kills the REQ-029 cue and the REQ-020 acceptance) passes the whole suite, and TEST-2717 remains the source-scan REQ-029 itself bans; (c) App.tsx's root phoneRemoteStatus() pull-and-toast (the late-subscriber half of REQ-020) has NO pin at all (TEST-2718 pins only the push subscription); (d) the FINDING-058/060 burst-queue interleavings (flushNow-before-barrier, markStale-mid-attach) are exercised nowhere through the real service seam — the session tests explicitly bypass the burst layer. The fix guidance on all five findings requested these pins; the human ESC-003 decision authorized the budget-conscious fix mode with 'frozen suite + re-review still arbitrate' — but re-review arbitrates once, while an unpinned fix can regress forever after.
fix: Run a tests-phase amendment (the sanctioned frozen-suite loopback path) adding: a restart-issuance vector (fresh instance + persisted-only state + token GET asserts Set-Cookie, then a cookie-only WS upgrade succeeds); an assertion in TEST-2653/2710 that status().enabled stays true after a failed start; a renderer test pinning the App.tsx root status-pull toast on a carried error; service-seam interleaving vectors for subscribe-racing-a-parked-chunk and saturation-during-attach. propose CONV: A loopback fix for a blocking finding MUST land with a test pinning the finding's vector (via a tests-phase amendment) in the same round — a fix arbitrated only by re-review is unpinned and can silently regress.
location: tests/ (frozen suite unchanged across the ESC-003 fix round — implement gate token 'test files unchanged since freeze') vs src/main/phone-remote/service.ts v3

claim: The v3 late-subscriber recovery ghost-toasts a stale error after the user has deliberately disabled the feature: lastError is cleared ONLY by a successful attemptStart, never by an explicit disable — so after enable-fails (port occupied) then setEnabled(false), status() keeps reporting { enabled: false, running: false, error: 'Phone remote could not start on port …' } indefinitely, and App.tsx's root recovery pull toasts st.error WITHOUT checking st.enabled. Every subsequently created window (undock spawns a fresh renderer root) and every window reload re-raises an error-severity toast — which persists until manually dismissed (error toasts never auto-dismiss) — for a feature the user explicitly turned OFF, telling them to free a port they no longer care about. The case the v3 fix ignored: 'error present' is not the same state as 'error present AND the feature is still wanted'. A disabled feature is the spec's inert state (REQ-001) — it should surface nothing.
fix: Clear lastError in doSetEnabled's disable branch (and/or in stop()) — a deliberate disable resolves the failure state; alternatively gate App.tsx's root pull-toast on st.enabled (the REQ-020 acceptance vector is persisted enabled:true). Cover with a vector: enable-fail, disable, assert status().error is absent (or the root consumer stays silent).
location: src/main/phone-remote/service.ts:539-546 (doSetEnabled disable branch — lastError untouched) + src/renderer/App.tsx:166-168 (unconditional root pull-and-toast)

claim: The FINDING-058/060 exactly-once repair is entirely unpinned by the suite: the ESC-003 fix commit (c39dba3) changed no test files (test-freeze held through the fix round), and no test traverses the burst-layer ordering it introduced — flushNow/markStale appear nowhere under tests/ (grepped), TEST-2625/2705/2706 drive createWsSession directly and bypass the burst queue (the exact insufficiency FINDING-058/060 documented), and the real-transport TEST-2713 saturates without interleaving output against a barrier capture. Consequence: deleting the load-bearing flushNow() call at service.ts:409 (message handler) or :322 (pong handler), or reverting the saturation discard from session.markStale back to the old paneData(id,'') substitute, silently regresses REQ-009/REQ-017 exactly-once through the production path with the whole suite green. REQ-009's amended acceptance ('repeated with output racing the subscribe call') and REQ-017's interleaved-drain vector are exercised only at the session seam that round-2 review proved insufficient.
fix: Tests-phase amendment adding the service-level vectors FINDING-058/060 already demanded, through the REAL connection path (the TEST-2713 harness style): (1) feed a chunk and process a subscribe frame for that pane in the same tick, assert the reconstruction contains the chunk exactly once (in the snapshot XOR as data); (2) flood past PHONE_WS_HIGH_WATER during an in-flight attach/resync and assert a later pong-driven drain emits a resync containing the discarded bytes; (3) a paneExit with a same-tick parked final chunk still delivers that chunk before the exit push.

claim: grid (and status) pushes bypass the per-connection burst queue, so a desktop resize's grid message can overtake pane data fed in the same tick that is still parked in burstQueue: onGrid -> session.paneGrid -> safeSend transmits immediately while the parked pre-resize bytes wait for the next macrotask/flushNow — the client resizes its xterm to the new cols/rows BEFORE applying up to one macrotask of pre-resize output, transiently mis-wrapping it (the shared mirror applies the same bytes in the correct order, data then resize, so the client and mirror renderings diverge until the post-resize full repaint or a resync). Self-healing in practice (a real desktop resize always provokes a ConPTY repaint) and sub-millisecond window; no REQ is violated (REQ-010 pins only per-pane DATA order), but it is the one cross-message-class ordering inversion the burst queue introduced, and the paneExit path already got the flush-first treatment for exactly this reason (service.ts:475). Filed once at LOW per calibration.
fix: Flush the connection's burst queue before emitting a grid push (entry.flushNow() in the onGrid fan-out, mirroring the paneExit treatment), or route grid pushes through the same per-connection queue so data/grid relative order matches main's observed order.
location: src/main/phone-remote/service.ts:378-393 (queueData parks data) vs :478-481 (onGrid fan-out sends grid immediately)

claim: The stall-episode clock in the v3 transport watch is wall-clock and is never reset per pending episode, so the forceTerminate decision is a function of clock adjustments and a 50ms scheduling race rather than the connection's actual behavior. (a) Episode carryover: pendingSince = Date.now() is set once when the watch first arms and cleared only in the pong handler (when pending fully resolves after socketDrained) or by a timer tick that observes no pending — but unsubscribe/paneExit clear staleness synchronously WITHOUT resetting it, so a NEW pending episode beginning within the <=50ms armed-tick window after the previous one resolved inherits the OLD episode's pendingSince, and pendingTooLong (line 308) can forceTerminate a healthy connection up to almost the full PHONE_WS_STALL_TIMEOUT_MS early. (b) Non-monotonic source: Date.now() jumps with NTP steps and system sleep/resume — a forward jump while ANY backpressure state is pending (or while stallPolicy.aboveSince is set, since onBuffered/stalledPast ride the same wall clock) spuriously trips the 60s ceiling on the first tick after resume and kills a healthy connection. Consequence is bounded — the client auto-reconnects with a lossless fresh attach per REQ-024 — but the phone visibly drops to 'reconnecting' at a moment determined by the host's clock, not by the protocol.
fix: Reset pendingSince at every pending->non-pending transition (clear it wherever session.hasPending() can go false — the unsubscribe/paneExit frame paths — or simply re-derive it inside armTransportWatch by checking hasPending() at arm time rather than only timer presence), and switch pendingSince plus the stallPolicy onBuffered/stalledPast timestamps to a monotonic source (performance.now() / process.hrtime.bigint()) so suspend/NTP can never manufacture a stall.

claim: REQ-031's documented Tailscale pairing path is still unreachable even with a valid bare externalHost (distinct from FINDING-068's malformed-input case): docs/features/phone-web-remote.md:17-27 tells users to keep the server on localhost, expose it through tailscale serve HTTPS, set only the tailnet hostname, and scan. But src/main/phone-remote/service.ts:174-186 treats externalHost as only a hostname and always emits http://<externalHost>:<backend settings.port>/?token=...; PhoneRemoteSettings.tsx:150-162 reinforces that hostname-only input. Tailscale Serve's public origin is HTTPS (normally port 443) reverse-proxied to localhost:<backend port>, so http://tailnet-host:<backend port> bypasses the proxy and cannot reach a listener bound to 127.0.0.1. Thus the ESC-001 decision/REQ-031 MUST that pairing be reachable under every documented access story was not actually applied; the existing ranking/copy UI tests prove string construction, not reachability.
fix: Model the advertised PUBLIC ORIGIN, not only a host: accept and validate an external origin such as https://my-machine.tailXXXX.ts.net (or separate external scheme/port fields), build the pairing URL with URL APIs without forcing the backend scheme/port, and update docs/features/phone-web-remote.md to the current tailscale serve command. Add a test where backend http://127.0.0.1:8199 is advertised as https://host/ and the pairing URL/QR preserves that external origin while bind remains localhost.
resolution: Fixed in the ESC-004 round (commit 50b240c) BEFORE this re-file: service.ts:183-187 externalOrigin() parses a full http(s) origin from externalHost and buildPairingUrl (:199) emits it verbatim (scheme preserved, no backend port) — the re-filed claim quotes the pre-fix code at lines that no longer exist. Settings affordance + doc walkthrough updated in the same commit. Verified by the full frozen suite (353/353) and both e2e specs post-fix; the re-filing cross-check never re-read the fixed code.

claim: REQ-017/REQ-026 permanently lose a pane's final output when it exits while that client is stale/saturated. In src/main/phone-remote/service.ts:456-467 output is fed to the mirror then parked per connection; on exit, service.ts:469-475 disposes the mirror BEFORE flushNow()/session.paneExit(). flushNow only repairs an under-threshold parked chunk. If ws.bufferedAmount is already above high water, deliverData drops the flushed final chunk and marks stale; if the burst queue itself saturated, the real chunks were already discarded and only stale remains. ws-session.ts:227-233 then clears stale/resync state and sends paneExit, while the only fresh snapshot source was just disposed. The promised drain resync can never occur and reconnect cannot attach an exited pane, so command-ending diagnostics can disappear permanently. This is distinct from FINDING-079's missing regression pin and its under-threshold parked-chunk vector.
fix: Before disposing the mirror, capture a final barrier snapshot for every exiting pane and make each tracking session finalize in wire order with a buffer-replacing final resync/snapshot followed by paneExit, then clear stale state; retain the mirror until that bounded finalization completes. Preserve the per-client memory ceiling (or explicitly terminate with a surfaced incomplete-history state if the final snapshot cannot be queued). Add real-service vectors for ordinary socket-high-water and pre-transport burst saturation immediately followed by pane exit, asserting the client's final terminal equals mirror truth before the exit indication.
resolution: Fixed in the ESC-005 round (commit 50b240c) BEFORE this re-file: ws-session.ts:250-268 paneExit detects an outstanding delivery obligation (hold window or stale, computed post-flushNow) and delivers a final write-flush-barrier snapshot as a buffer-replacing resync before paneExit/exited; service.ts:501-507 defers mirrors.paneExited until every session's exit fan-out settles (taps-identity re-check). The re-filed claim cites the pre-fix disposal order. Verified by the full frozen suite + a real-WebSocket saturation vector delivering the final diagnostic chunk.

claim: REQ-009/REQ-013 mirror ordering is not preserved when output is pending during a desktop resize (distinct from FINDING-080, which covers the client-side burst queue). replay-engine.ts:103-109 feeds with asynchronous term.write(data, callback), but resizePane reaches replay-engine.ts:112-115 and calls term.resize synchronously with no write barrier; service.ts:456-480 composes those two paths. Therefore a resize can overtake already-observed pane bytes inside the authoritative mirror, so later attach/resync snapshots parse pre-resize bytes at the new grid instead of matching the desktop's data-then-resize order. A direct probe against the shipped @xterm/headless code confirmed it: at 5x2, feed('ABCDE12345') then resize(10,2) before the write callback serializes as one unwrapped line, while waiting for the callback before the same resize produces the expected reflow/control sequence. FINDING-080's proposed client flush cannot repair a snapshot whose server mirror is already wrong.
fix: Put feed and resize in one ordered replay-operation domain: a resize requested with writes pending must run inside the callback/barrier after all previously issued writes and before any subsequently issued writes. Add an actual headless replay test that compares feed-before-resize and resize-before-feed against reference terminals, plus a service attach/resync vector with a resize racing output.
resolution: Fixed: mirror resize rides the write-ordering FIFO (replay-engine.ts:126-142) — synchronous only when parsedSeq >= issuedSeq (queue provably empty), otherwise enqueued as a PendingOp behind the pending write chain and applied in observation order via drain(), invalidating the serialize cache across an interleaved resize. Snapshot barrier semantics unchanged. Verified: full frozen suite + gate.

claim: REQ-006's regenerate-is-atomic MUST fails on persistence error. src/main/phone-remote/service.ts:590-600 mutates the live settings.tokenHash and sessionToken BEFORE awaiting deps.saveSettings(), but closes existing sockets and returns the new pairing URL only AFTER that await. If quick.json persistence rejects, the IPC call reports failure while main has silently switched future HTTP/WS auth to an undisclosed new token, existing old-token sockets remain open, disk still contains the old hash (which resurrects after restart), and pairingUrl() can later expose a token from an operation the UI was told failed. tests/main/phone-remote-service.test.ts:305's atomicity test covers only the success path. This is a real partial commit across memory, disk, clients, and renderer state.
fix: Stage candidate settings/token without mutating live state; await persistence first, and only on success synchronously publish settings + sessionToken, close all sessions, and return the URL. On save failure leave the old in-memory token/hash/session set untouched. Add an injected saveSettings rejection vector asserting the old credential and live client remain coherent and no new token becomes observable; retain the existing success-path revoke-all vector.
resolution: Fixed: regenerate is persist-first (service.ts:643-660) — candidate token staged, saveSettings awaited BEFORE any live mutation; publish (settings+sessionToken), socket revocation, and URL return only on save success; a rejected save leaves live state byte-identical. Verified: full frozen suite 353/353 + implement gate re-derivation.

claim: REQ-003/REQ-019 lifecycle/settings mutations are also partially committed when quick.json persistence fails (distinct from FINDING-085's REQ-006 regenerate transaction). service.ts:532-574 mutates live settings and, for setEnabled, starts/stops the transport before awaiting saveSettings; setBind/setPort mutate settings before save and restart only after it. A rejected setBind therefore leaves status().bind reporting LAN while the live listener is still localhost (computeUrls advertises unreachable LAN URLs), directly violating REQ-019's 'reported state and actual listening state can never diverge'; failed enable/disable can change the live listener while disk retains the opposite intent, so restart reverses an IPC operation that was reported as failed. No failure path rolls back or stages state.
fix: Use staged candidate settings for every operation. Persist the candidate before publishing it to live status or changing the transport; after persistence succeeds, synchronously publish then perform the serialized start/stop/rebind (preserving enabled:true + runtime error on bind failure). If persistence rejects, retain the old settings/listener exactly. Add saveSettings-rejection vectors for enable, disable, bind, and port that assert status, actual socket reachability, and persisted intent remain coherent.
resolution: Fixed: settings lifecycle is persist-first (service.ts:580-627) — setEnabled/setBind/setPort/setExternalHost stage a candidate, await saveSettings, then publish + apply transport change only on success; REQ-019 reported-vs-actual divergence on save failure eliminated. FINDING-063/071 runtime-failure semantics preserved (startIfEnabled untouched). Verified: full frozen suite + gate.

claim: The ESC-004/005 fix round (commit 50b240c) shipped two significant new code paths with zero test coverage, the same un-pinned-fix pattern FINDING-077/079 already flagged for ESC-003. (1) service.ts's externalOrigin() full-http(s)-origin branch (service.ts:183-187, the actual FINDING-082 fix) is untested: TEST-2695 (tests/main/phone-remote-service.test.ts:631-643) only exercises the pre-existing bare-hostname override ('myhost.ts.net'); no test ever passes an externalHost starting with https:// and asserts the pairing URL preserves that scheme/host/port with NO backend port appended (grep of tests/ for 'https://'+'externalOrigin'+'tailscale' returns zero hits in any phone-remote test file). Reverting buildPairingUrl to always use pairingHost()+backend-port would pass the whole suite green. (2) ws-session.ts paneExit's owesFinal branch (lines 250-268, the actual FINDING-083 fix) has NO test anywhere: every existing paneExit test (TEST-2631, and the 'a subscribed pane exit pushes paneExit and the terminal exited status' test) only exercises a LIVE, non-stale pane, hitting the synchronous !owesFinal path. No test marks a pane stale (or leaves it mid-attach/resync) and then calls paneExit to assert a final resync is delivered, in wire order, before paneExit/status:exited. Deleting the entire owesFinal block and its final-snapshot delivery would pass the whole suite green, silently regressing the exact REQ-017/REQ-026 exactly-once guarantee ESC-005 was raised to fix.
fix: Tests-phase amendment adding: (a) a case asserting a https://host:port externalHost yields a pairing URL with that exact scheme/host/port and no backend port appended, alongside the existing bare-hostname case, so a scheme-stripping regression fails fast; (b) a ws-session test that saturates/holds a pane (flood past high-water, or leave it mid-attach) then calls paneExit(), asserting the returned promise resolves after a resync containing the dropped/held bytes is sent BEFORE the paneExit/status:exited pushes.

claim: Accessibility: the phone client's dynamically-shown message surfaces are silent to assistive tech — #error-banner (REQ-030's mandated error surface) and #connection-status (which carries the TERMINAL 'pairing revoked — scan the new QR code' state, REQ-024) are plain divs toggled via hidden/textContent with no role/aria-live, so a server error frame or the revoked-pairing terminal state renders visually but is never announced to a screen-reader user; the revoked state additionally rides an 11px #888 strip (the lowest-salience text on the page) despite being the one state that halts the whole client and requires user action. The desktop half of this same feature already uses role=alert for its warning/validation surfaces (PhoneRemoteSettings.tsx), so the client is inconsistent with the feature's own established pattern. Distinct from FINDING-067 (the Ctrl button's aria-pressed) — different elements, different defect.
fix: Add role=alert (implicit aria-live assertive) to #error-banner and role=status (aria-live polite) to #connection-status in index.html; give the terminal revoked state higher visual salience than the routine connected/reconnecting strip (e.g. route it through the error banner instead of the status strip).

claim: The pane-exit overlay defeats the final-output guarantee the feature just paid for: .terminal-exit-overlay is a full-cover layer (position:absolute; inset:0; z-index:5) at rgba(20,0,0,0.72) shown permanently when the actively-viewed pane exits, with no dismiss affordance — it visually obscures (72% opaque dark red over the whole grid) and touch-intercepts exactly the command-ending output that ESC-005/FINDING-083's final-barrier-snapshot machinery guarantees is delivered. The last lines of output are precisely what a user checks when a process exits (exit status, error text, build result), and once the REQ-011 membership push removes the exited pane's list row there is no way back to that content at all — the only escapes are Back (losing the view) or another pane. REQ-030 is met to the letter (in-view notice + input disabled) but the presentation buries the content the notice is about.
fix: Render the exit notice as a non-covering strip (e.g. a banner above the key bar or under the back button) instead of a full-cover overlay, or make the overlay tap-to-dismiss / pointer-events:none with the notice docked to one edge — keep input disabled either way (the 'exited' state, not the overlay, should gate input).

claim: The phone-remote HTTP server (service.ts handleRequest / static-assets.ts serveStaticFile) sends no hardening response headers on any served content -- no X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, no Content-Security-Policy, no X-Frame-Options/frame-ancestors. The served index.html is DOM-safe today (pane-list.ts/main.ts insert every server-derived string via .textContent, never innerHTML, and no addon-web-links is loaded), but nothing at the transport layer backstops that invariant against a future change, and nothing prevents the authenticated page from being framed by a third-party site for clickjacking -- SameSite=Lax on the REQ-028 session cookie is the only defense against a cross-site iframe carrying it, and FINDING-061 already documents that SameSite enforcement is an unpinned browser implementation detail (e.g. inconsistent on iOS Safari). No test in the suite asserts any response header beyond content-type/content-length.
fix: Add X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff to every response in handleRequest/serveStaticFile, and either X-Frame-Options: DENY or Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'none' (optionally default-src 'self'; script-src 'self', consistent with REQ-021's self-contained-bundle invariant) on the '/' response. Add a unit test asserting these headers on a served route.

claim: CONV-018 violation: the ESC-004 and ESC-005 human decisions changed REQ-required behavior but were never propagated into 02-spec.md (spec-freeze token still reports 'spec unchanged since the spec gate' as a PASS while the spec is now stale). (1) REQ-031(b)'s normative text ('an optional external host override (externalHost ... a hostname, not a secret)') and acceptance ('with externalHost set, the decoded QR/pairing URL uses that host and the configured port') contradict the shipped v4 behavior, where a full http(s) origin builds the pairing URL VERBATIM — https scheme preserved, the origin's own port (default 443), the configured backend port never appended — so the spec's own acceptance vector now FAILS against the sanctioned implementation. (2) REQ-017's stale-state lifecycle ('a drain never resyncs a pane no longer subscribed/live', resync 'hold-window-ordered') and REQ-026 ('final output already delivered flows through the normal stream'; the mirror 'is disposed on pane exit') omit the v4 exit-time final buffer-replacing resync delivered for a no-longer-live pane and the deferred, settle-gated mirror disposal. The v2 loopback itself established the discipline (the spec's Amendment history exists per CONV-018); the ESC-004/005 round encoded the decisions in code + feature doc only, so a future maintainer or re-reviewer reading REQ-031/REQ-017/REQ-026 re-derives the pre-decision design and would flag the shipped behavior as a violation.
fix: Run the sanctioned spec-amendment path (a v3 Amendment history entry citing ESC-004/ESC-005): amend REQ-031(b) to the hostname-OR-full-origin override with origin-verbatim pairing-URL semantics (scheme preserved, no backend port), and amend REQ-017/REQ-026 acceptance to include the exit-time final-snapshot resync for an owed pane + mirror disposal deferred until every session's exit fan-out settles. No code change required — the implementation is the human-settled truth; the spec must catch up.
