claim: The ESC-004/005 round fixes (commit 50b240c) shipped with zero regression pins, extending the FINDING-077/079 unpinned-fix class to two NEW human-mandated exactly-once/reachability surfaces: (1) WsSession.paneExit's owesFinal branch (the FINDING-083 final-output-at-exit guarantee, REQ-017/REQ-026) appears in no test — 'owesFinal' matches nothing under tests/, the only paneExit backpressure references are comments in phone-remote-backpressure.test.ts, and the service-level exit fan-out's deferred mirror disposal (Promise.all settle + taps re-check, service.ts:501-508) is untraversed; deleting the whole owesFinal branch or reverting to dispose-before-fan-out regresses the ESC-005 HIGH fix with the suite green. (2) The FINDING-082 external-ORIGIN pairing path (externalOrigin(), buildPairingUrl origin-verbatim branch) is untested — TEST-2695 presents only a bare hostname ('myhost.ts.net'); no test presents an https:// origin, so deleting externalOrigin() regresses the ESC-004 HIGH fix (Tailscale pairing unreachable again) with the suite green. The fix round held test-freeze, so the gate's test-freeze token structurally cannot catch this recurrence.
fix: Tests-phase amendment pinning both round-4 fixes: (a) a ws-session/service vector — subscribe, saturate (markStale or an unresolved attach window), then paneExit — asserting the client receives resync (containing the discarded bytes) BEFORE paneExit/status-exited, and that mirror disposal is deferred until the settle promise resolves; and the no-obligation path stays synchronous. (b) a service vector with settings.externalHost = 'https://machine.tailXXXX.ts.net' asserting the pairing URL is the origin verbatim (https scheme preserved, no backend port appended) and that a bare hostname keeps http + backend port. propose CONV: a loop-back fix round for a blocking finding MUST land at least one regression test traversing the fixed path in the same round (test-freeze is amended, not held) — an unpinned fix is an unfinished fix.
location: src/main/phone-remote/ws-session.ts:250-268 (owesFinal final-delivery branch), src/main/phone-remote/service.ts:183-205 (externalOrigin) vs tests/ (no coverage)
src/main/ipc/register.ts:174 (onResize else-branch; sibling hazard at :111) + src/main/ipc/register-pty.ts:139/143 (unconditional onResize fan-out)
claim: REQ-011 inventory currency is broken by an event-order hole: a pty:resize arriving AFTER pty:exit resurrects the exited pane as a permanent ghost. The exit handler deletes the pane from phoneRemoteLivePanes (register.ts:115), but the renderer keeps an exited pane MOUNTED under the dead-pane overlay (TerminalPane.tsx:351, the M-3 restart affordance) and its ResizeObserver/grid-sync path has no exited guard — the load-bearing fit-must-pair-with-ptyResize rule guarantees a pty:resize fires for the exited pane on the next window resize / layout change. register-pty.ts invokes deps.onResize unconditionally (lines 139/143; PtyManager.resize deliberately swallows the dead-pty throw), and the register.ts onResize fallback re-adds the unknown id: phoneRemoteLivePanes.set(id, {cols, rows, status: idle}) at line 174. Because paneMeta was deleted at exit, the ghost lands in a phantom group (workspaceId unassigned, name Workspace, fallback title). The next membership push (any workspace autosave fires pushInventoryToAll) then lists a nonexistent pane as an idle terminal on every connected phone: tapping it attaches to an empty snapshot (mirror disposed at exit, so readSnapshot yields an empty string) with dead input; ensureTap on the next membership event builds a leaked 2000-line headless mirror for it; and NOTHING ever removes the entry — user-closing the exited pane emits no second pty:exit, and disable/enable does not clear the map; only an app restart does. Ordinary reproduction: exit a shell, leave the exited overlay up, resize the window. This contradicts the amended REQ-011 MUST (the inventory MUST stay current; a removed pane leaves — here it RE-ENTERS) and silently undoes the exit-leaves-the-list behavior TEST-2711 pins (the pin never interleaves a post-exit resize).
fix: Drop the resurrection fallback: on an unknown paneId, onResize must NOT create a phoneRemoteLivePanes entry (replace the else-branch with a return; the spawn/adopt paths already seed real entries with real workspaceId). Audit the sibling ptyData fallback at register.ts:111 the same way (guard on a known pane or re-verify liveness) since it has the identical resurrection shape if any data ever trails an exit. Add a regression vector: spawn, exit, then fire a resize for the exited id and assert the next inventory push does not re-list it.
resolution: Fixed: onResize no longer upserts (register.ts:185-188) — unknown/exited pane ids return without creating an inventory entry or fanning out grid events; sibling ptyData guard (:117) stops trailing data minting a ghost mirror tap. Desktop renderer + register-pty.ts untouched (fit/ptyResize pairing preserved). Verified: full frozen suite + gate.

location: src/main/phone-remote/mirror-manager.ts:51-53 (registerPane replaces without disposing) + src/main/phone-remote/service.ts:506 (presence-based disposeExited re-check)
claim: Residual resource leak inside the FINDING-083 deferred-dispose fix, on the exact race its own re-check defends: mirror-manager registerPane does panes.set(info.paneId, rec) unconditionally, so re-registering a paneId whose record still exists REPLACES the record without disposing the previous mirror. During the exit-settle window (mirror disposal deferred until every session paneExit promise settles), a tap re-registration for the same paneId — the very case the disposeExited comment contemplates — orphans the old record: disposeExited then sees taps.has(paneId) true and skips mirrors.paneExited, and that call would anyway have targeted the successor record, so the superseded @xterm/headless Terminal (up to 2000 lines of scrollback plus its serialize addon) is never disposed for the life of the process. The guard is presence-based (taps.has) rather than identity-based (the repo session-identity pattern re-checks that the claimed slot IS the captured one), which is why the superseded resource escapes both dispose paths. Reachability is narrow (requires same-paneId re-registration within the milliseconds-scale settle window, which normal pty data-before-exit ordering prevents), so this is a hygiene/leak defect, not a correctness one — the in-flight final snapshot is captured synchronously before any replacement can occur, so exactly-once delivery is unaffected.
fix: Make registerPane dispose (or refuse and reuse) an existing record for the same paneId before replacing it, or capture the Registered record reference at exit fan-out time and have disposeExited dispose exactly that captured record (identity, not presence) — either closes the orphan on every interleaving.

claim: REQ-003 persistence is defeated by two unsynchronized whole-record quick.json writers. The renderer loads quick once at startup (src/renderer/store.ts:293-297), while PhoneRemoteSettings deliberately keeps its state outside that store (src/renderer/components/PhoneRemoteSettings.tsx:18-26) and the service independently merges phoneRemote into the current disk record (src/main/ipc/register.ts:201-205). Every later renderer save sends its stale full QuickStore (store.ts:71-73), and the mandatory quit flush always does so (App.tsx:132-137); register-workspaces.ts:37-41 passes that stale payload to QuickStore.save, which replaces the whole normalized file (quick-store.ts:59-62). Thus enable, bind, port, externalHost, or a freshly regenerated tokenHash can be erased by an ordinary renderer settings save or app shutdown; the next launch reverts the feature and a persisted cookie/token generation is lost. This is distinct from FINDING-085/086: those cover rejection inside one service operation, while this succeeds on disk and is later clobbered by a separate owner.
fix: Give phoneRemote one coherent field owner: make renderer quick saves merge only renderer-owned fields with the latest main-side record while preserving phoneRemote, or update a serialized main-side quick-store API that performs field-scoped atomic mutations for both writers. Do not send tokenHash to the renderer merely to refresh its snapshot. Add an integration test: load a renderer snapshot, enable/regenerate through Phone Remote, then issue saveQuick with the stale snapshot and perform the quit flush; reload quick.json and assert enabled/bind/port/externalHost/tokenHash all survive.
resolution: Fixed: main is the single owner of quick.json's phoneRemote field — the quick:save IPC handler (register-workspaces.ts:48-57) overlays the authoritative main-side state (live persist-first service settings via a main-only currentSettings() accessor; disk-record fallback under the e2e seam so harness state never persists) over any renderer whole-record payload. Renderer-owned fields unchanged; nothing phoneRemote-related sent to the renderer. Proven by a behavioral self-check driving the REAL handler+QuickStore+service (stale save with no phoneRemote while enabled+paired → persisted file retains enabled/tokenHash/externalHost, renderer favoriteDirs change lands; 11/11 assertions) plus the full frozen suite (353/353) and gate re-derivation.

claim: The shipped PWA icons are placeholder pixels, not the dimensions advertised by the manifest. Direct PNG IHDR inspection shows src/phone-client/icons/icon-192.png and icon-512.png are each only 68 bytes and 1x1 pixels, while manifest.webmanifest:11-13 declares them as 192x192 and 512x512 and index.html:16 uses the 1x1 file as the iOS apple-touch-icon. User agents may ignore a manifest image whose intrinsic dimensions contradict sizes, and an installed iPhone shortcut receives an effectively blank one-pixel icon. TEST-2677 only checks that the directory is nonempty (tests/phone-client-build.test.ts:78-86), so these placeholders pass the claimed PWA-install-assets test.
fix: Replace the placeholders with real non-secret 192x192 and 512x512 PNG assets (and preferably a dedicated Apple touch icon). Extend TEST-2677 to parse PNG IHDR dimensions and assert they match every manifest sizes declaration and the intended touch-icon size.

claim: The phone client leaks one status entry per pane ID for the lifetime of a connected page. PaneList.setStatus always inserts into its private statuses Map (src/phone-client/pane-list.ts:31-45), including the mandatory exited update from main.ts:124-129, but setInventory only adds current pane IDs and never deletes IDs absent from the new authoritative inventory. Because exited panes leave inventory and pane IDs are not reused, a long-running phone session that observes repeated pane creation/exit grows this Map without bound; the retained entries are invisible after their rows disappear but never become collectible.
fix: Treat each panes inventory as authoritative for membership: rebuild statuses from the incoming workspaces (or delete all keys not present) before rendering. Add a unit test that sets a status for pane A, replaces inventory with pane B, and verifies A is no longer retained, using a behavior-facing accessor or a bounded-size invariant rather than exposing mutable internals.

claim: REQ-023's subscription-hygiene switch (unsubscribe the departing pane before subscribing the next) is wired in production ONLY as a partial, silent consumption of a plan abstraction with zero test coverage at the wiring level. src/phone-client/ws-client.ts's paneSwitchPlan(from, to) returns BOTH an unsubscribe step (for the departing pane) and a subscribe step (for the arriving pane), and is unit-tested (tests/phone-client-core.test.ts:197-204) as if both steps were live. But main.ts's actual pane-select handler (main.ts:76-90) silently discards the subscribe step -- 'for (const step of paneSwitchPlan(prev, paneId)) { if (step.type === 'unsubscribe') send(step) }' -- with no comment explaining why, because a real subscribe must instead flow through TerminalView.open()/openPanePlan() (which sizes the terminal from the freshest grid BEFORE subscribing, per REQ-013). This filtering behavior is completely unexercised by any test: the pure paneSwitchPlan unit test only checks the function in isolation, not that main.ts forwards just the unsubscribe half; the DOM e2e (tests/e2e/phone-remote-client-dom.spec.ts, TEST-2731) only ever opens ONE pane and never switches to a second, yet its own header comment (line 12) claims '...the departing pane is unsubscribed' as covered -- a false coverage claim, the same class already flagged once in this feature (FINDING-055); and the raw-WebSocket e2e (phone-remote.spec.ts) bypasses main.ts's bundle entirely, connecting with  directly. REQ-023's own acceptance text explicitly requires 'switching pane A -> pane B emits unsubscribe{A}' as mandated coverage (added via the ESC-001 loopback specifically to close a residual-obligation gap), yet no test -- unit, DOM e2e, or wire e2e -- ever drives a real two-pane switch through the served client and asserts the unsubscribe frame. A regression (e.g. someone 'simplifying' the loop to forward every step from paneSwitchPlan, or inverting the filter) would send a premature subscribe before the terminal is sized, silently reintroducing the exact mis-wrapped-pane bug REQ-013 exists to prevent, and the full suite would still pass green.
fix: Add a wiring-level test: either (a) extend TEST-2731's DOM e2e to open pane A, switch to pane B, and assert (via a raw WS listener or server-side hook) that unsubscribe{A} is sent before subscribe{B}, matching REQ-023's stated acceptance verbatim, or (b) a focused unit test that imports main.ts's onSelect logic (refactored into an exported pure function taking {prev, next, send} if needed for testability) and asserts it forwards exactly the unsubscribe step from paneSwitchPlan and never the subscribe step. Also correct or substantiate the TEST-2731 header comment's 'the departing pane is unsubscribed' claim, or remove it until true.

claim: The phone inventory's group ordering and fallback pane labels ride incidental map state rather than a stated stable rule: (1) buildInventory orders workspace groups by first-pane-encounter order in deps.panes.list(), which is [...phoneRemoteLivePanes.entries()] insertion order = pane spawn/adoption order — so the phone's workspace order matches neither the desktop's workspace order nor any stated ranking, and reshuffles across app restarts; (2) humanTitle's 'kind N' fallback index (kindIndex) is recomputed from scratch over the CURRENT list on every inventory push, so when an earlier untitled sibling exits, every later untitled pane in that workspace is silently RENAMED one index down ('terminal 2' becomes 'terminal 1') on the very push that also removes a row — inventory.ts's own comment claims 'the numbering stays stable as more panes are added', which holds only for additions, not removals; a user mid-tap can select a row that just renamed AND shifted. Both are deterministic per event history but are derived identity/order as a function of Map insertion order and current membership — the same class (unordered/incidental collection state feeding a user-facing payload) REQ-031 closed for URL ranking.
fix: Order workspace groups by a stated deterministic key (workspace name then id, or thread the desktop's real workspace order into the inventory), and make fallback titles stable per pane lifetime: assign 'kind N' once at spawn (persist it in phoneRemotePaneMeta / the live-panes record) instead of recomputing the index per push, so a sibling's exit never renames surviving panes.

claim: Regenerate pairing token is a one-click destructive action with no confirmation: a single (mis)click immediately replaces the token, invalidates every outstanding session cookie, and terminates every connected phone (closeAllSessions) — every paired device must physically re-scan a QR to recover. REQ-007's disclosed-consequence text renders as static copy BELOW the button (easily unread), and the app's own v0.18.0 QoL convention is confirm-before-destructive for far less costly actions (pane/workspace close confirms). The spec's letter (disclosure) is met, so this is a consistency/friction gap, not a REQ violation.
fix: Gate the regenerate behind a lightweight confirm (the app's existing confirm pattern / a two-step 'Regenerate…' -> 'Really revoke all paired devices?' button swap), keeping the consequence text as the confirm body.

claim: The pairing-URL Copy button silently swallows clipboard failure and bypasses the app's established copy chokepoint: it calls navigator.clipboard?.writeText with .catch(() => {}) — on failure (or when navigator.clipboard is undefined, where the optional chain makes .then throw on undefined and nothing at all happens) the user gets zero feedback, the button just stays 'Copy'. Every other copy affordance in the app (ExplorerPane copy-path, EditorTabStrip copy-path) rides api.clipboardWrite (the main-process Electron clipboard, robust against renderer clipboard-permission quirks) plus a pushToast confirmation. CONV-001's surfacing half: a failed user action must not be silent — and this environment demonstrably has intermittent global-clipboard contention, so the failure path is real, not theoretical.
fix: Route the copy through api.clipboardWrite like every other copy site (add it to the injected slice deps or call it directly), and surface failure — pushToast on error, keeping the inline 'Copied!' success cue. Guard the navigator.clipboard-undefined case if the web API is kept.

claim: The full-screen terminal view renders NO pane identity: #view-terminal contains only the '← Panes' back button, the terminal, and the key bar — the active pane's title and workspace name (which the inventory already delivers, and which main.ts already looks up via paneList.getPane at select time) are shown nowhere. After switching among several similar panes (the exact multi-pane workflow the workspace-grouped list exists for), the user must remember which pane is live; typed input — including key-bar Ctrl+C — lands in an unlabeled terminal, so misdirected destructive input into the wrong pane is plausible. REQ-023 scoped the v1 UI minimally, so this is a spec-scope polish gap, not a REQ violation.
fix: Render the active pane's title (and optionally workspace name) in the terminal view's header row next to the back button — the data is already in hand at open() time. propose CONV: A full-screen detail view reached from a list of similar items MUST display the selected item's identity in its own chrome, never relying on the user's memory of which row was tapped.

claim: REQ-014 ('no filesystem or editor access', 'no pane lifecycle actions') is enforced only by an unchecked, untested, cross-module invariant, not by any explicit kind check in the phone-remote authorization path. ws-session.ts's doSubscribe/doInput authorize a client's subscribe/input purely via deps.panes.isLive(paneId), which service.ts wires to deps.panes.list().find(p => p.paneId === paneId) over register.ts's phoneRemoteLivePanes map. That map is populated unconditionally by registerPty's onSpawn/onResize callbacks (register-pty.ts lines 107/127, invoked from the pty:spawn/pty:resize IPC handlers) with NO pane-kind parameter at all -- it has no way to exclude a non-terminal pane even in principle. Today it only ever contains terminal-kind panes because pty:spawn is only called from TerminalPane.tsx (confirmed by grep: no other renderer component calls ptySpawn) -- but that is an emergent fact about a DIFFERENT module (the renderer), not an invariant enforced or even checked anywhere in the phone-remote surface itself. Contrast with REQ-013 (no resize), which the same file's own docblock says 'the session cannot reach what it cannot see, so REQ-013/REQ-014 hold structurally, not just behaviorally' -- true for resize (WsSessionDeps exposes no resize capability at all) but NOT true for the editor/filesystem-access half of REQ-014, which relies entirely on an unenforced, untested assumption elsewhere in the codebase. No test anywhere (phone-remote-inventory.test.ts, phone-remote-session.test.ts, or a composition-root test) asserts that isLive/write reject a non-terminal-kind pane id -- buildInventory's kind==='terminal' filter (inventory.ts) only affects what appears in the LISTING a client sees, not what paneId a client may subscribe/write to. A future pane kind that legitimately spawns a PTY (or any future code path that inserts into phoneRemoteLivePanes for a non-terminal pane) would silently grant every phone-remote client full read/write access to it, with zero change needed on the phone-remote side and no test catching the regression.
fix: Add an explicit kind check at the authorization boundary itself: either (a) have registerPty's onSpawn/onResize accept and store the spawning pane's kind so phoneRemotePaneRecord can report it and service.ts's paneInfo/isLive path can reject non-terminal kinds before deps.panes.isLive ever returns true, or (b) assert the invariant structurally with a composition-root test analogous to phone-remote-structure.test.ts's REQ-013 guard, e.g. a test that spawns a synthetic non-terminal-kind pane through the SAME phoneRemoteLivePanes-populating callback and asserts a subscribe/input for it is rejected. Prefer (a): move the guarantee into the code path an untrusted network message actually reaches, matching the REQ-013 structural pattern this same module already uses.

claim: The ESC-006 fix round (commit f71553b) is the THIRD consecutive fix round shipped with zero regression pins, and its four vectors are NOT covered by the existing unpinned-round findings (FINDING-077 is scoped to ESC-003's vectors, FINDING-087/092 to commit 50b240c): (1) FINDING-085's transactional regenerate — no test rejects deps.saveSettings during regenerateToken and asserts the old token/cookie still authenticate and connected clients stay open; (2) FINDING-086's persist-first settings — no test rejects a save in setEnabled/setBind/setPort and asserts live state (listening, status(), the actual listener) is byte-identical; (3) FINDING-084's resize-joins-the-write-barrier FIFO in replay-engine.ts — REQ-009's core exactly-once machinery gained a whole new ordering domain (PendingOp queue, cache invalidation on interleaved resize, dispose dropping pending resizes) with NO interleaving vector (feed-then-resize-before-parse-then-snapshot), so reverting resize() to synchronous passes the entire suite; (4) FINDING-093's no-upsert gates in register.ts — no test drives pty:resize (or pty:data) after pty:exit through the registrar and asserts the ghost pane stays out of the inventory. The implement gate's test-freeze token ('test files unchanged since freeze') is itself the proof: the frozen suite cannot distinguish the fixed code from the code all four blocking findings were filed against, so any of the four can silently regress forever after. Each finding's closure rests solely on one round of re-review reading.
fix: Extend the tests-phase amendment FINDING-077 already demands to cover the ESC-006 vectors: a saveSettings-rejection vector for regenerateToken (old credential still valid, sessions open) and for setEnabled/setBind/setPort (live state unchanged); a replay-engine interleaving vector (feed A at grid G1, resize to G2 before A's write callback, feed B, snapshot — assert the reference-terminal reconstruction at G2 with A pre-reflow); a registrar-level vector that pty:resize/pty:data after pty:exit re-lists nothing. Same CONV proposal as FINDING-077 applies (a loopback fix MUST land with its pin in the same round).
location: commit f71553b (ESC-006 round) — src/main/phone-remote/replay-engine.ts, service.ts persist-first paths, src/main/ipc/register.ts data/resize gates; tests/ unchanged since freeze

claim: Third instance of the unpinned-fix class (extends FINDING-079/092 to the ESC-006 round): commit f71553b's human-mandated fixes shipped with test-freeze held and zero regression pins. (1) FINDING-084 ordered mirror resize: no test imports src/main/phone-remote/replay-engine (grepped, zero matches under tests/); the exactly-once reference-terminal oracles in tests/main/phone-remote-session.test.ts build their mirrors from src/agent/replay, which per replay-engine.ts's own header deliberately KEEPS the original synchronous resize - so reverting the production resize() to synchronous (undoing the FINDING-084 HIGH: pre-resize bytes reflowed at the new grid in attach/resync snapshots) leaves the whole suite green. (2) FINDING-085/086 persist-first transactions: every saveSettings stub in tests/main/phone-remote-service.test.ts is 'async (s) => { stored = s }' - none ever rejects, so re-ordering back to publish-then-persist (or half-committing live state on a rejected save: status().bind reporting LAN while still bound to localhost, or live auth switched to a token whose hash never landed on disk) regresses both fixes with the suite green. (3) FINDING-093 resurrection guard (register.ts onResize no-upsert + mirror-manager resizePane early-return): lives in the composition root no unit test traverses - a pty:resize after pty:exit resurrecting a permanent inventory ghost is unpinned.
fix: Tests-phase amendment (freeze amended, not held) pinning the round-5 fixes: (a) a unit test importing src/main/phone-remote/replay-engine directly - feed bytes, call resize() before the write callback has run, then snapshot(), asserting the serialize reflects data-then-resize order (the FINDING-084 reflow vector), plus the fast path (resize with nothing pending stays synchronous); (b) a service vector whose saveSettings rejects once - assert setBind/setPort/setEnabled/regenerateToken each leave status(), live auth (old token still accepted, connected client still open), and the listener byte-identical; (c) an inventory vector - spawn, exit, then a late grid change for the exited id, asserting it does not reappear in list() or any push. FINDING-092's proposed CONV (a loop-back fix round must land at least one regression test traversing the fixed path in the same round) covers this class going forward - this is its third instance in one feature.
location: src/main/phone-remote/replay-engine.ts:126-142, src/main/phone-remote/service.ts:570-660, src/main/ipc/register.ts:177-189 vs tests/ (zero traversal of the ESC-006 fixes)

claim: Cross-check of FINDING-072: its MEDIUM platform premise is stale/unreproduced for the current declared iPhone-Safari PWA target. The install flow in features/0026-phone-web-remote/02-spec.md:451-464 and src/phone-client/manifest.webmanifest:5 relies on cookie transfer at Add to Home Screen; WebKit's Safari 17.2 release notes explicitly state iOS/iPadOS copies the browser's cookies, including login state, into the new Home Screen web app. The separate container therefore does not lose the cookie on current iOS. Only pre-17.2 compatibility remains, and the spec declares no legacy-iOS support floor.
fix: Close FINDING-072 as unreproduced against current WebKit, or explicitly add a pre-iOS-17.2 support floor and fallback if legacy devices are intended; do not treat historical container separation alone as a current MEDIUM defect.

claim: FINDING-068 is materially stale after the settled ESC-004 decision. A value carrying an http(s) scheme is no longer a malformed 'bare host' paste: src/shared/phone-remote/settings.ts:17-21 and src/main/phone-remote/service.ts:183-210 intentionally accept a full origin, and src/renderer/components/PhoneRemoteSettings.tsx:150-165 tells users to paste the HTTPS origin from tailscale serve. The residual defect is narrower: arbitrary non-origin strings such as whitespace-bearing hosts or bare host:port still persist silently and can build an invalid URL. FINDING-068's proposed bare-host-only validation would regress the human-settled Tailscale path.
fix: Refine FINDING-068 to validate either a bare hostname or a syntactically valid http(s) origin, with a specific UI error for anything else; do not reject schemes or normalize a valid Tailscale HTTPS origin down to a hostname.

claim: REQ-007/REQ-031 pairing becomes stale after ordinary bind or port changes. src/renderer/store/phone-remote-slice.ts:85-100 applies the returned status for setPhoneRemoteBind/setPhoneRemotePort but never re-fetches phoneRemotePairingUrl; only setExternalHost does so at :103-109. PhoneRemoteSettings renders the cached phoneRemotePairingUrl at src/renderer/components/PhoneRemoteSettings.tsx:173-184. Thus generate on localhost, switch to LAN, and the QR/copyable URL still encodes 127.0.0.1; changing the port likewise leaves the old port. The service already recomputes from current settings in src/main/phone-remote/service.ts:662-665, but the UI does not request it until remount, so the advertised pairing surface is unreachable in the normal toggle workflow.
fix: After successful setBind and setPort (and any enabled transition that changes the actual bound endpoint), call phoneRemotePairingUrl and update phoneRemotePairingUrl exactly as setExternalHost does; clear it on unavailable. Add a slice/DOM or e2e vector that generates on localhost, flips LAN and port, and decodes/asserts the displayed URL uses the new reachable host and port without regenerating the token.
resolution: Fixed: setPhoneRemoteBind/setPhoneRemotePort re-fetch the pairing URL after applying status (phone-remote-slice.ts:94-121), byte-identical to the existing setExternalHost injected-dep pattern. Verified: targeted + full frozen suite (353/353) + gate.

claim: REQ-015's desktop-forward timing guarantee is inverted in the REAL composition. src/main/ipc/register.ts:108-130 invokes every phoneRemoteDataListener before the original send(channel,...args). The production listener at src/main/phone-remote/service.ts:479-492 synchronously looks up/registers a mirror, feeds @xterm/headless, iterates every session, allocates/byte-counts burst entries, and only then returns so renderer pty:data can be sent. Although mirror-manager.ts:51-59 was designed/tested to call its forward callback first, service.ts:164-167 and :483-486 register production mirrors with a no-op forward, bypassing that guarantee. Therefore enabling the feature puts all mirror/fan-out CPU work ahead of desktop IPC delivery, violating the MUST that renderer bytes/order/timing semantics remain unchanged and that a slow client never delay that path.
fix: In phoneRemoteSend, call the real renderer send first and only then notify the observational phone listeners (with containment so listener failures never propagate), or compose mirror-manager with the real forward callback rather than a no-op. Add a production-composition test recording call order and asserting renderer send occurs before mirror feed/session queue work with the feature enabled and clients attached.
resolution: Fixed: phoneRemoteSend reordered (register.ts:108-136) — the original renderer send runs FIRST, then the phone-remote listener fan-out inside a containment try/catch; REQ-015 desktop-forward timing restored; FINDING-093 known-pane guard preserved verbatim. No frozen test pinned the old order (checked mirrors/e2e-seam/service pins first). Verified: full frozen suite + both e2e specs + gate.

claim: Refinement of FINDING-108: cross-window token regeneration leaves an already-mounted second Settings panel showing a revoked QR. register-phone-remote.ts:82-85 returns the new pairing URL only to the invoking window and broadcasts status without the URL; src/renderer/components/PhoneRemoteSettings.tsx:50-58 feeds that broadcast to ingestPhoneRemoteStatus, whose implementation at src/renderer/store/phone-remote-slice.ts:56-65 updates only status. The second window's cached phoneRemotePairingUrl at PhoneRemoteSettings.tsx:173-184 therefore remains token A after another window regenerates token B, even though token A is immediately invalid. This is not repaired by FINDING-108's local setBind/setPort refresh alone.
fix: On a phoneRemote:changed event indicating token/session availability, re-fetch phoneRemotePairingUrl and replace or clear the cached URL in every mounted panel; alternatively include a safe generation identifier that triggers the pull. Add a two-slice/window test: seed both with token A, regenerate through one, deliver the status broadcast to the other, and assert the other no longer renders A and fetches/renders B.
resolution: Fixed: regenerate's broadcast now carries a secret-free pairingUrlChanged:true bump (register-phone-remote.ts:88, status.ts:21) and ingestPhoneRemoteStatus re-pulls the URL on it (phone-remote-slice.ts:66-73) — a second mounted Settings panel refreshes its QR without any token/URL riding the push. Verified: full frozen suite + gate.

claim: FINDING-101's core silent-failure concern is valid at src/renderer/components/PhoneRemoteSettings.tsx:95-101, but its claimed undefined-clipboard exception is technically wrong: navigator.clipboard?.writeText(...).then(...).catch(...) is one continuous optional chain and evaluates to undefined without throwing when clipboard is absent (confirmed under the repository's Node runtime). The real behavior is a silent no-op when absent and a swallowed rejection when present-but-failing, not an uncaught .then-on-undefined failure.
fix: Keep FINDING-101 scoped to missing user feedback and the bypassed Electron clipboard path; route through api.clipboardWrite and toast failures, but do not justify it with a nonexistent optional-chaining exception.

claim: FINDING-103 overstates a hypothetical future hardening gap as a current MEDIUM security defect. The live-pane map is populated only from the PTY registrar's onSpawn path at src/main/ipc/register-pty.ts:84-128, and the only renderer production calls to ptySpawn are TerminalPane.tsx:110-114 and :204-208. Non-terminal editor/explorer/orky/notes panes therefore have no current entry in the map and cannot be subscribed to or written through ws-session. An explicit terminal-kind check would be useful defense-in-depth against a future PTY-backed non-terminal kind, but FINDING-103 identifies no present REQ-014 violation within the frozen feature's declared pane model.
fix: Downgrade/close FINDING-103 as speculative future-proofing, or retain it only as LOW hardening with a structural invariant test; reopen as a behavior/security defect only when a non-terminal production path can actually enter phoneRemoteLivePanes.

claim: REQ-027's documented anywhere-access recipe uses obsolete Tailscale CLI syntax. docs/features/phone-web-remote.md:17-20 tells users to run 	ailscale serve https / http://127.0.0.1:8199, but Tailscale's current Serve reference (last validated 2026-01-26) says the CLI changed in v1.52 and now accepts 	ailscale serve [flags] <target>; its reverse-proxy examples are 	ailscale serve localhost:3000 / 	ailscale serve 3000. Following the shipped command on a current client therefore does not establish the spec's only documented encrypted anywhere-access path, despite features/0026-phone-web-remote/02-spec.md:583-593 requiring a working tailscale serve recipe with pairing steps.
fix: Replace the obsolete command with current syntax such as 	ailscale serve localhost:8199 (or 	ailscale serve --bg localhost:8199 for persistence), explain that the printed HTTPS origin goes into External host/origin, and pin the current command form in the REQ-027 docs test.
resolution: Fixed: docs/features/phone-web-remote.md walkthrough updated to current Tailscale Serve syntax (tailscale serve localhost:8199, --bg note, v1.52 migration note); FINDING-082's explicit-origin pairing guidance intact; docs-feature-0026 pin still satisfied. Verified: docs test green in targeted run + full suite.

claim: CONV-011 class: phoneRemoteWorkspaceNames (the workspace-id -> name map added by the round-2 REQ-011 fix, register.ts:80-84) grows monotonically for the life of the app — onPhoneRemoteWorkspaceSaved only ever .set()s it, the deleteWorkspace IPC handler (register-workspaces.ts:43) has no phone-remote hook, and no dispose-clear exists — so every workspace ever saved keeps its (uuid, name) entry after deletion. The sibling phoneRemotePaneMeta map IS pruned (on pty:exit and on each ws:save of the owning workspace), which makes the omission look like an oversight rather than a decision. Impact is small (one string pair per deleted workspace per session; uuid keys can't re-enter, so no stale-serving) but it is a per-key cache mirroring a live membership set with no pruning for keys that leave the set — exactly the CONV-011 rule.
fix: Thread a workspace-deleted signal into the phone-remote wiring (add an onWorkspaceDeleted callback beside onWorkspaceSaved in registerWorkspaces deps, call it from the CH.deleteWorkspace handler) and have it delete the phoneRemoteWorkspaceNames entry plus any phoneRemotePaneMeta entries still keyed to that workspaceId; alternatively prune inside onPhoneRemoteWorkspaceSaved by sweeping names against store.listWorkspaceIds().

claim: The ESC-004 round's new settings copy renders markdown-style backticks as literal characters in the UI: the external-host help paragraph shows `tailscale serve` and `https://my-machine.tailXXXX.ts.net` with raw backtick glyphs (JSX renders them as text), and the localhost-reachability disclosure shows a `tailscale serve` hostname the same way — while the SAME component's LAN warning correctly uses tailscale serve markup, so the panel is inconsistent with itself and the pairing-critical copy looks like an unrendered template.
fix: Replace the literal backticks in the two paragraphs with ... elements matching the LAN warning's existing pattern.

claim: Residual nondeterministic two-writer race on quick.json survives the FINDING-095 overlay fix: the writers are now field-coherent but still unserialized, so any overlapped save's on-disk outcome is a function of fs rename completion order. (a) Stale-overlay direction: the quick:save handler reads currentSettings() synchronously at IPC arrival, but every service persist-first op publishes settings = candidate only AFTER its own saveSettings resolves — a renderer save (debounced settings save or the App.tsx quit flush) arriving while a service op's write is in flight overlays the PRE-op settings and races its atomicWrite against the service's; the later-starting (renderer) rename typically lands last, and atomic-write.ts's Windows transient-EPERM retry loop (up to 10 retries, 10-100ms backoff, provoked by exactly this concurrent same-file access) shuffles the order further. When the stale write wins: regenerateToken leaves the REVOKED token's hash on disk while live auth uses the new token — a restart resurrects the revoked credential and orphans the just-paired device (the exact REQ-006 both-directions atomicity FINDING-085 fixed for the sequential case) — or a successful Enable persists as disabled (REQ-003), e.g. toggle-then-quit where the quit flush races the in-flight setEnabled persist. (b) Reverse direction (never protected by the overlay): the service's saveSettings is an unserialized whole-record read-modify-write (quick.load() then quick.save({...cur, phoneRemote: s}), register.ts:218) — a renderer save landing in that load-to-save window has its renderer-owned fields (a just-added SSH favorite, theme, keybindings) silently reverted by the service's write built from the pre-save snapshot. FINDING-095's resolution self-check drove a SEQUENTIAL stale save; no test exercises either concurrent interleaving.
fix: Serialize every quick.json write through one owner: give QuickStore (or the composition root) a single per-file write queue and route BOTH the quick:save handler's persist and the service's saveSettings through it as queued load-merge-save steps; have the overlay read currentSettings() INSIDE its queued step (after any in-flight service op has published) so the overlaid value can never be pre-publish stale. Add a concurrent vector: begin setEnabled/regenerateToken against a slow-resolving save, fire quick:save mid-flight, and assert the final on-disk record carries both the new phoneRemote AND the renderer's field change. propose CONV: Every writer of one persisted file MUST share a single serialized write queue; an unserialized read-modify-write against a file another path also writes is a finding even when each individual write is atomic.
location: src/main/ipc/register.ts:216-218 (saveSettings load-merge-save) + src/main/ipc/register-workspaces.ts:49-53 (overlay read at handler entry) + src/main/persistence/atomic-write.ts (last-rename-wins)
src/main/ipc/register-workspaces.ts:47 (CH.quickLoad handler) + src/renderer/store.ts:296 vs the ESC-007 decision's second clause
claim: The ESC-007 human decision's fix shape had two clauses: 'single field owner - the main-side quick:save path preserves main-owned phoneRemote fields over any renderer whole-record payload; no tokenHash sent to the renderer.' Only the first was implemented. CH.quickLoad still returns quick.load() unfiltered, so every renderer receives the full phoneRemote object INCLUDING tokenHash into zustand state at startup (store.ts init: api.loadQuick() -> set({quick})) and on every loadQuick refresh. Meanwhile the shipped record asserts the opposite: currentSettings()'s doc comment says 'NEVER exposed over IPC - the renderer must not receive tokenHash or any other phoneRemote secret material', and commit 191ba69's message says 'nothing phoneRemote-related is sent to the renderer'. Direct security impact is small (the hash is preimage-resistant and the REQ-028 cookie value is the plaintext token, not derivable from the hash), but a settled human decision's explicit clause was not applied while the code and commit claim compliance a future reader will trust - and the three-layer model keeps secret material in main precisely because renderer state is the least-trusted surface.
fix: Strip phoneRemote from the quickLoad response in register-workspaces.ts (the renderer has no consumer of it - PhoneRemoteSettings rides phoneRemote:status, and the FINDING-095 overlay already makes the renderer's phoneRemote copy irrelevant on save); or, if exposure is consciously accepted, correct the currentSettings()/commit claims and record the deviation against ESC-007 in the followups doc (per ESC-008 routing).

location: commit 191ba69 (ESC-007/008 round) - src/main/ipc/register-workspaces.ts:48-57 overlay + register.ts phoneRemoteSettings accessor; tests/ unchanged since freeze
claim: The ESC-007/008 fix round (commit 191ba69) is the FOURTH consecutive fix round shipped with zero test changes (the implement gate's own token: 'test files unchanged since freeze'), and its vector is not covered by the existing unpinned-round findings (FINDING-077 scopes ESC-003, FINDING-087/092 scope 50b240c, FINDING-104/105 scope f71553b): no repository test drives a renderer whole-record quick:save (or the quit flush) through the real CH.quickSave handler with a phoneRemoteSettings accessor wired and asserts the persisted record keeps main's phoneRemote - deleting the FINDING-095 overlay from register-workspaces.ts/register.ts passes the entire frozen suite, so the exact clobber the human called 'functionally broken, not a v1 limitation' (a settings save or quit flush erasing enable/bind/port/tokenHash, invalidating every device pairing) can silently regress forever. The commit message's 'behavioral self-check driving the real handler+store+service chain' was a one-off, not a checked-in test. FINDING-116's concurrent interleavings are additionally untested, but even the SEQUENTIAL fixed vector has no pin.
fix: Extend the tests-phase amendment FINDING-077/104 already demand with an overlay vector: register the real quickSave handler with a phoneRemoteSettings accessor, drive a stale whole-record renderer payload through it, and assert the persisted record preserves main's phoneRemote (enable/bind/port/tokenHash) while renderer-owned fields take the payload's values; add FINDING-116's concurrent interleavings alongside. Same CONV proposal as FINDING-077 (a loopback fix MUST land with a test pinning the finding's vector in the same round).

claim: REQ-017 status/grid latest-wins ordering breaks when the socket buffer falls below high water before the drain probe runs. createBackpressurePolicy.onPush stores an old saturated push, but a newer push observed with bufferedAmount at or below highWater returns true and is sent immediately without replacing or clearing that held entry. WsSession.socketDrained later emits the older held entry after the newer wire message. Concrete vector: hold status busy at 101 bytes, observe status idle at 0 bytes (idle sends), then drain/take emits busy, reverting the phone to stale state; grid can similarly revert dimensions. TEST-2702/2708 keep every replacement above high water and miss the threshold-crossing interleaving.
fix: When a held kind+paneId entry already exists, onPush must replace it with the new payload and continue returning false until takeHeldPushes delivers it, regardless of the current bufferedAmount; alternatively delete the prior held entry before allowing the newer push through. Add policy and session tests for hold-old-above-high, push-new-below-high-before-drain, drain, asserting only the new payload is emitted and wire order never regresses.

claim: REQ-030's stopped-input exit state is undone by reconnect. main.ts handles paneExit by TerminalView.setExited(), but leaves activePaneId set; the next WebSocket open unconditionally calls terminalView.open(info ?? fallback). TerminalView.open resets exited=false, hides the overlay, and sends subscribe. Because the subsequent REQ-011 inventory push has removed the exited pane, info is normally absent and the 80x24 fallback specifically reopens the dead id. The server rejects the subscribe/input, but the phone has already hidden 'process exited' and resumed accepting/sending keystrokes. The same path mishandles a pane that exits while the socket is disconnected. Existing tests only inspect setExited statically and never interleave paneExit/disconnect/reconnect.
fix: Preserve an explicit active-pane-exited state across reconnect, and do not call TerminalView.open for it. More generally, wait for the reconnect attempt's fresh panes inventory before re-attaching the active pane: if the active id is present, open using its authoritative grid; if absent, keep/set the exited overlay and do not subscribe or accept input. Add a DOM-level paneExit -> close/open and disconnected-exit -> reconnect inventory test asserting the overlay remains, no subscribe/input for the dead id is sent, and selecting a different live pane clears the state.

claim: REQ-011/REQ-026 are violated on a synchronous local PTY launch failure: PtyManager.spawn catches node-pty.spawn errors, emits onData and onExit synchronously, then returns. registerPty calls pty.spawn first and unconditionally invokes deps.onSpawn afterward. Thus phoneRemoteSend sees the failure data/exit while phoneRemoteLivePanes has no entry (drops phone data, deletes nothing), then the late onSpawn inserts the already-exited id as idle and broadcasts membership. The failed pane is now a permanent live-inventory ghost: phone subscribe passes isLive and receives an empty mirror, input silently reaches PtyManager.write's missing-session no-op, and no second exit will remove it. This is a distinct current resurrection path from the fixed post-exit resize upsert in FINDING-093.
fix: Establish the observational phone-remote spawn record before calling PtyManager.spawn, matching tracker.register's existing failure-order discipline, so the synchronous failure data is mirrored and the synchronous exit deletes the entry and fan-outs paneExit/inventory removal; or make spawn report failure and explicitly avoid/roll back onSpawn while preserving the exit/error sequence. Add a composition test with node-pty.spawn throwing that asserts no live pane/mirror remains, inventory excludes the id, and subsequent input gets the specific exited/no-such-pane error.
